tool_input.content:
# Hospital Referral (ส่งตัวรักษาพยาบาลแบบไม่ต้องสำรองจ่าย) - Full Stack Implementation Plan

## Feature Overview
Cashless hospital referral workflow: Employee requests a referral letter → Manager approves → HR Admin issues the letter → Employee uses it at a hospital (no out-of-pocket payment).

## Workflow Steps
1. **Employee** creates a hospital referral request (selects hospital, reason, preferred date)
2. **Manager** reviews and approves/rejects (1st level)
3. **HR Admin** issues the referral letter (2nd level)
4. Status updates: draft → submitted → pending_manager → pending_hr → approved → letter_issued
5. Employee can view/download the referral letter

## Implementation Tasks

### Task 1: Backend - Prisma Schema & DTOs (benefits-management service)
**Files:**
- `src/services/benefits-management/prisma/schema.prisma` — Add `HospitalReferral` model
- `src/services/benefits-management/src/referral/dto/create-referral.dto.ts`
- `src/services/benefits-management/src/referral/dto/referral-filter.dto.ts`
- `src/services/benefits-management/src/referral/dto/update-referral.dto.ts`

**HospitalReferral model:**
```
- id, employee_id, employee_name
- hospital_name, hospital_branch
- reason (illness description)
- preferred_date, valid_from, valid_until
- status: draft | submitted | pending_manager | pending_hr | approved | rejected | cancelled
- workflow_id (link to workflow engine)
- referral_number (generated on approval, e.g., REF-2026-0001)
- approved_by, approved_at
- issued_by, issued_at
- rejected_by, rejected_reason
- notes, created_at, updated_at
```

### Task 2: Backend - Referral Service & Controller
**Files:**
- `src/services/benefits-management/src/referral/referral.service.ts`
- `src/services/benefits-management/src/referral/referral.controller.ts`
- `src/services/benefits-management/src/referral/referral.module.ts`

**Endpoints:**
- `POST /referrals` — Create referral request
- `GET /referrals` — List with filters (employee, status, date range)
- `GET /referrals/:id` — Get by ID
- `PATCH /referrals/:id/submit` — Submit for approval (triggers workflow)
- `PATCH /referrals/:id/approve` — Manager/HR approve
- `PATCH /referrals/:id/reject` — Reject with reason
- `PATCH /referrals/:id/issue` — HR issues the referral letter
- `PATCH /referrals/:id/cancel` — Employee cancels

### Task 3: Workflow Engine - Add `hospital_referral` route
**Files:**
- `src/services/workflow-engine/src/workflow/workflow.service.ts` — Add `hospital_referral` to APPROVAL_ROUTES (2-level: manager + hr_admin)
- `src/services/workflow-engine/src/rules/rules.service.ts` — Add to `managerAndHr` set

### Task 4: Backend Unit Tests
**Files:**
- `src/services/benefits-management/test/referral.service.spec.ts`

**Test cases (~25-30 tests):**
- Create referral (valid, missing fields)
- Submit referral (draft only, not found)
- Approve/reject (authorization, state transitions)
- Issue letter (only after approved, generates referral number)
- Cancel (only pending states)
- Filter/list (by status, date, employee)
- Edge cases (duplicate pending, expired referral)

### Task 5: Frontend - Hook & Mock Data
**Files:**
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-hospital-referral.ts` — Mock hook with CRUD, status transitions
- Hospital list mock data (Bangkok Hospital, Bumrungrad, BNH, Samitivej, etc.)

### Task 6: Frontend - Hospital Referral Page & Components
**Files:**
- `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/hospital-referral/page.tsx` — Main page with tabs
- `src/frontend/src/components/hospital-referral/referral-request-form.tsx` — Multi-step form
- `src/frontend/src/components/hospital-referral/referral-history.tsx` — History table
- `src/frontend/src/components/hospital-referral/referral-detail.tsx` — Detail modal with timeline
- `src/frontend/src/components/hospital-referral/referral-letter-preview.tsx` — Letter preview/print

**Page structure:**
- Tabs: New Request | My Referrals | Referral Letter
- Form: Hospital selection → Reason/Date → Review & Submit
- History table with status badges, filters
- Detail modal with approval timeline
- Letter preview with print button

### Task 7: Frontend - Sidebar, Routing & i18n
**Files:**
- `src/frontend/src/components/shared/sidebar.tsx` — Add hospital referral item to selfService
- `src/frontend/messages/en.json` — Add hospitalReferral.* keys
- `src/frontend/messages/th.json` — Add Thai translations

**i18n keys:**
```
hospitalReferral.title: "Hospital Referral" / "หนังสือส่งตัว"
hospitalReferral.newRequest: "New Referral Request" / "สร้างใบส่งตัวใหม่"
hospitalReferral.myReferrals: "My Referrals" / "ใบส่งตัวของฉัน"
hospitalReferral.referralLetter: "Referral Letter" / "หนังสือส่งตัว"
hospitalReferral.selectHospital: "Select Hospital" / "เลือกโรงพยาบาล"
hospitalReferral.reason: "Reason for Visit" / "เหตุผลในการรักษา"
hospitalReferral.preferredDate: "Preferred Date" / "วันที่ต้องการ"
hospitalReferral.validUntil: "Valid Until" / "ใช้ได้ถึง"
hospitalReferral.referralNumber: "Referral Number" / "เลขที่ใบส่งตัว"
hospitalReferral.status.*: Status labels in both languages
```

### Task 8: Playwright E2E Test
**File:** `tests/e2e/hospital-referral.spec.ts`

**Test scenarios (~8-10 tests):**
1. Navigate to hospital referral page
2. View empty referral list
3. Create new referral request (fill form, submit)
4. View referral in history with "submitted" status
5. View referral detail modal with approval timeline
6. Test form validation (required fields)
7. Test hospital dropdown selection
8. Test cancel functionality
9. Verify i18n switching (EN → TH)
10. Mobile responsive layout check

## Execution Order
1. Tasks 1-3 (Backend) — can be parallelized
2. Task 4 (Backend tests)
3. Tasks 5-7 (Frontend) — can be parallelized
4. Task 8 (E2E tests)

tool_input.prompt:
## Task: Mobile UI Phase 6 — E2E Visual Verification

Take screenshots of all key pages at mobile width (375px) to verify the mobile UI improvements.

### Setup
The app is running at http://localhost:3000. Use Playwright MCP browser tools.

1. First, install browser if needed: `mcp__playwright__browser_install`
2. Navigate to http://localhost:3000/en/home
3. Resize browser to 375x812 (iPhone SE/13 mini size): `mcp__playwright__browser_resize` width=375, height=812

### Screenshots to take

Take screenshots at 375px width for each page. Save each screenshot using bash `mv` to the target directory.

**Screenshot directory:** `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/agents/mobile-ui/phase6/`
Create the directory first: `mkdir -p /Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/agents/mobile-ui/phase6/`

For each page:
1. Navigate to the URL
2. Wait for content to load (use `mcp__playwright__browser_wait_for` if needed)
3. Take a screenshot with `mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot`
4. Move the screenshot to the target directory with a descriptive name

### Pages to test (in order):

1. **Home page** → http://localhost:3000/en/home
   - Screenshot: `01_home_mobile.png`
   - Verify: Cards should show icon + title only (no descriptions), compact padding

2. **Mobile menu** → Open by clicking the hamburger menu button (☰)
   - Screenshot: `02_mobile_menu.png`
   - Verify: Should show user info at top, grouped sections, logout at bottom

3. **Leave page** → http://localhost:3000/en/leave
   - Screenshot: `03_leave_mobile.png`
   - Verify: Leave balance cards should NOT show progress bars

4. **Payslip page** → http://localhost:3000/en/payslip
   - Screenshot: `04_payslip_mobile.png`
   - Verify: Compact card padding

5. **Profile page** → http://localhost:3000/en/profile
   - Screenshot: `05_profile_mobile.png`
   - Verify: Employee ID hidden, email truncated, buttons full-width

6. **Workflows** → http://localhost:3000/en/workflows
   - Screenshot: `06_workflows_mobile.png`
   - Verify: Descriptions truncated to 1 line, action buttons stacked

7. **Smart Claims** → http://localhost:3000/en/smart-claims
   - Screenshot: `07_smart_claims_mobile.png`
   - Verify: Compact upload zone, step indicator fits on screen

8. **Hospital Referral** → http://localhost:3000/en/hospital-referral
   - Screenshot: `08_hospital_referral_mobile.png`

9. **Test a modal** — On any page with a button that opens a modal (e.g., leave request), click it
   - Screenshot: `09_modal_bottom_sheet.png`
   - Verify: Modal should appear as bottom sheet (rounded top corners, drag indicator)

10. **Quick Approve** → http://localhost:3000/en/quick-approve
    - Screenshot: `10_quick_approve_mobile.png`
    - Verify: Card list view (no table), checkboxes visible

### Report
After all screenshots, provide a summary of:
- Which pages look good on mobile
- Any issues found (overflow, text cut off, elements too small, etc.)
- Whether the bottom sheet modal works correctly

### IMPORTANT
- Always resize to 375x812 FIRST before navigating
- Wait for pages to load before taking screenshots
- If a page requires authentication or shows access denied, note it and move on
- Use `mcp__playwright__browser_snapshot` to check accessibility tree if visual issues found

tool_input.prompt:
Create the file `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/hooks/use-medical-claims.ts` with the following requirements:

This replaces the old `use-claims.ts` hook. Follow the same React hook patterns (useState, useEffect with setTimeout mock delays, useCallback for mutations).

Types to define:
- `DiseaseCategory` = 'general' | 'dental' | 'vision' | 'maternity' | 'accident' | 'chronic' | 'mental_health' | 'other'
- `MedicalClaimStatus` = 'draft' | 'submitted' | 'pending_manager' | 'pending_hr' | 'processing' | 'approved' | 'rejected'
- `ClaimDocument` = { id: string, type: 'receipt' | 'medical_cert' | 'other', fileName: string, fileSize: number, uploadedAt: string }
- `ApprovalStep` = { step: number, role: string, approverName: string, status: 'pending' | 'approved' | 'rejected', date: string | null, comment: string | null }
- `MedicalClaim` = { id, diseaseCategory: DiseaseCategory, diseaseDetails: string, hospitalName: string, treatmentDate: string, receiptAmount: number, claimAmount: number, remarks: string, documents: ClaimDocument[], approvalSteps: ApprovalStep[], status: MedicalClaimStatus, submittedAt: string | null, approvedAt: string | null, rejectionReason: string | null, rejectedAt: string | null, createdAt: string, updatedAt: string }
- `MedicalBenefitSummary` = { planType: string, annualLimit: number, usedAmount: number, remainingAmount: number }

Constants:
- `DISEASE_CATEGORIES` array with objects: { value: DiseaseCategory, labelEn: string, labelTh: string }
  Values: general(General Illness/เจ็บป่วยทั่วไป), dental(Dental/ทันตกรรม), vision(Vision/Eye Care / จักษุ), maternity(Maternity/การคลอดบุตร), accident(Accident/อุบัติเหตุ), chronic(Chronic Disease/โรคเรื้อรัง), mental_health(Mental Health/สุขภาพจิต), other(Other/อื่นๆ)

Mock data:
- `MOCK_SUMMARY`: planType 'OPD + IPD', annualLimit 60000, usedAmount 18500, remainingAmount 41500
- `MOCK_CLAIMS`: 6 medical claims with Thai hospital names (Bangkok Hospital, Thonglor Dental, Rutnin Eye, BNH Hospital, Samitivej, Paolo Hospital)
  - MCL001: general, Bangkok Hospital, 3500, approved, 2 approval steps both approved
  - MCL002: dental, Thonglor Dental Clinic, 5000, approved, 2 approval steps both approved
  - MCL003: vision, Rutnin Eye Hospital, 5000/5000, pending_manager, step 1 pending, step 2 pending
  - MCL004: accident, Samitivej Hospital, 4500/4500, submitted, step 1 pending, step 2 pending
  - MCL005: general, BNH Hospital, 2500/2500, rejected, step 1 approved, step 2 rejected with comment "Insufficient supporting documents"
  - MCL006: chronic, Paolo Hospital, 1500/1500, draft, no approval steps yet

  Each approved claim should have documents (receipt + medical_cert). Pending and submitted should also have documents. Draft can have empty documents.

Hook `useMedicalClaims()`:
- State: claims (MedicalClaim[]), summary (MedicalBenefitSummary), loading (boolean)
- useEffect: setTimeout 300ms to load MOCK_CLAIMS and MOCK_SUMMARY
- `createClaim(data: Omit<MedicalClaim, 'id' | 'documents' | 'approvalSteps' | 'status' | 'submittedAt' | 'approvedAt' | 'rejectionReason' | 'rejectedAt' | 'createdAt' | 'updatedAt'>)`: creates a new claim with 'draft' status, returns it
- `addDocument(claimId: string, doc: Omit<ClaimDocument, 'id' | 'uploadedAt'>)`: adds document to a claim
- `removeDocument(claimId: string, docId: string)`: removes document from a claim
- `submitClaim(claimId: string)`: sets status to 'submitted', adds submittedAt, adds 2 approval steps (Manager "Surachai P." and HR "Parichat S." both pending)
- `saveDraft(claimId: string)`: keeps as draft (just an update trigger)
- `stats` computed: { totalClaims, pendingClaims (submitted/pending_manager/pending_hr/processing), approvedClaims, remainingBalance: summary.remainingAmount }

Export all types, the DISEASE_CATEGORIES constant, and the useMedicalClaims hook.

Make sure to add 'use client'; at the top. Use clean TypeScript.

tool_input.description: Audit TM spec vs implementation
tool_input.prompt:
I need to audit the Time Management (TM) Self Service spec against what's already implemented. The spec has 3 roles:

**1. TM Employee Self Service:**
- Mobile Clock (Web Application): Clock In/Clock Out
- Leave Management: Leave Request, Cancel Leave (Unapprove), Cancel Leave for approved items (sends to workflow), View Leave Balance
- Time Management: Time Correction Request, OT Request
- Time Sheet: View Shift & Schedule, View Time Result
- Workflow & Status Tracking: View Pending Approve

**2. TM Manager Self Service:**
- Direct Manager (1 Level): Approve/Reject Leave Request, Approve/Reject Time Correction Request, Approve/Reject OT Request
- SPD: View/Edit (Time, OT, Schedule, Leave Document)
- HRBP: View Report

**3. Admin Self Service:**
- Mobile Clock: Manage Employee Geofencing
- Manage Approval Workflow
- Leave with documents (e.g., welfare) requires sending to HRBP → HRBP Approve (2-step: 1. Direct Manager, 2. HRBP)

Search these files/directories thoroughly (medium thoroughness):
1. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/time/` - all files
2. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/leave/` - all files 
3. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/hooks/use-time.ts`
4. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/hooks/use-leave.ts`
5. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/hooks/use-overtime.ts`
6. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/workflows/` - for workflow tracking
7. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/` - check what pages/routes exist for time, leave, overtime
8. `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/shared/sidebar.tsx` - to see nav items

For each feature in the spec, tell me:
- DONE: Feature exists with working UI
- PARTIAL: Some UI exists but incomplete
- MISSING: Not implemented at all

Be specific about what code/components exist.

tool_input.command: find /Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/app/\[locale\] -type f -name "page.tsx" | head -30
find /Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/hooks -name "*workflow*" -o -name "*approve*" | head -10
ls -la /Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/overtime/ 2>/dev/null | head -5

tool_input.args:
TM Self Service - Missing Features Implementation Plan

## Context
We're building a Time Management Self Service module for an HR system (Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind CSS 4.1, next-intl). The Employee Self Service features are ~95% done. We need to implement the remaining features for Manager Self Service and Admin Self Service roles.

## TM Spec (from screenshot):

### 1. TM Employee Self Service (DONE)
- Mobile Clock: Clock In/Clock Out ✅
- Leave Management: Leave Request, Cancel Leave, View Leave Balance ✅
- Time Management: Time Correction Request, OT Request ✅
- Time Sheet: View Shift & Schedule, View Time Result ✅
- Workflow & Status Tracking: View Pending Approve ✅

### 2. TM Manager Self Service (PARTIAL)
- Direct Manager (1 Level): Approve/Reject Leave Request ✅, Approve/Reject Time Correction Request ✅, Approve/Reject OT Request ✅ (all via Workflows page)
- SPD: View/Edit Time, View/Edit OT, View/Edit Schedule, View Leave Document ❌ MISSING
- HRBP: View Report ❌ MISSING

### 3. Admin Self Service (PARTIAL)
- Mobile Clock: Manage Employee Geofencing ⚠️ PARTIAL (view only, no admin config)
- Manage Approval Workflow ⚠️ PARTIAL (basic create only, no step/approver config)
- Leave with documents (welfare etc.) requires 2-step approval: 1. Direct Manager → 2. HRBP ⚠️ PARTIAL (structure exists, routing logic incomplete)

## Existing Architecture
- Frontend: src/frontend/ (Next.js with App Router)
- Components: src/frontend/src/components/
- Hooks: src/frontend/src/hooks/
- i18n: src/frontend/messages/en.json, th.json
- Pages: src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/
- RBAC: src/frontend/src/lib/rbac.ts (Employee, Manager, HR Admin, HR Manager roles)
- Workflows: src/frontend/src/hooks/use-workflows.ts
- UI Kit: Card, Badge, Button, Tabs, Modal, FormField, Skeleton, Table components

## Requirements
Create a comprehensive plan covering all 5 missing/partial features with implementation details, file paths, component structure, and i18n keys needed.

tool_input.content:
# Plan: TM Self Service - Missing Features Implementation

## Task Description
Implement the remaining features from the TM (Time Management) Self Service specification. The Employee Self Service is ~95% complete, but Manager Self Service and Admin Self Service have significant gaps. This plan covers 5 missing/partial features across 3 roles.

## Objective
Complete the full TM Self Service module covering all 3 roles (Employee, Manager, Admin) as specified in the TM Self Service requirements document, so the system provides end-to-end time management from employee clock-in through manager approval to admin configuration.

## Problem Statement
The current implementation covers Employee Self Service well (clock in/out, leave, time correction, OT, timesheet, workflows). However:
1. **SPD role** has no UI for viewing/editing team time data, OT, schedules, or leave documents
2. **HRBP role** has no reporting dashboard
3. **Admin** cannot configure geofencing zones or customize approval workflow steps
4. **2-step approval** (Manager -> HRBP) for document-required leaves is not wired up
5. **Cancel Leave** for already-approved items (sends to workflow) is not distinct from canceling pending ones

## Solution Approach
Build 5 new feature modules that integrate with the existing architecture:
- Extend `use-workflows.ts` for 2-step approval routing and `time_correction` workflow type
- Create new SPD page under manager-dashboard for team time data management
- Create HRBP report dashboard with summary analytics
- Extend Settings page with Geofencing admin and Workflow configuration panels
- Enhance leave history to support cancel-approved-leave via workflow submission

## Relevant Files
Use these files to complete the task:

### Existing Files to Modify
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-workflows.ts` - Add `time_correction` type, 2-step approval logic for document leaves
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-time.ts` - Add team attendance data for SPD view, extend mock data
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-leave.ts` - Add cancel-approved-leave function that creates workflow
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-overtime.ts` - Add team OT data for SPD view
- `src/frontend/src/lib/rbac.ts` - Add `spd-management`, `hrbp-reports` module access
- `src/frontend/src/components/leave/leave-history.tsx` - Enhance cancel to handle approved leaves
- `src/frontend/src/components/shared/sidebar.tsx` - Add new nav items for SPD, HRBP reports
- `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/settings/page.tsx` - Add geofencing and workflow config tabs
- `src/frontend/messages/en.json` - Add all new i18n keys
- `src/frontend/messages/th.json` - Add all new Thai i18n keys

### New Files to Create
- `src/frontend/src/components/spd/spd-management-page.tsx` - SPD team data management page
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-spd.ts` - Hook for SPD team data (time, OT, schedule, leave docs)
- `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/spd-management/page.tsx` - SPD route page
- `src/frontend/src/components/hrbp/hrbp-reports-page.tsx` - HRBP reporting dashboard
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-hrbp-reports.ts` - Hook for HRBP report data
- `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/hrbp-reports/page.tsx` - HRBP route page
- `src/frontend/src/components/settings/geofencing-config.tsx` - Admin geofencing zone management
- `src/frontend/src/components/settings/workflow-config.tsx` - Admin approval workflow configuration
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-geofencing.ts` - Hook for geofencing zone CRUD
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-workflow-config.ts` - Hook for workflow template configuration

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation (Data Layer + RBAC)
- Create new hooks with mock data: `use-spd.ts`, `use-hrbp-reports.ts`, `use-geofencing.ts`, `use-workflow-config.ts`
- Update `rbac.ts` with new module permissions
- Extend `use-workflows.ts` with `time_correction` type and 2-step approval routing
- Add all i18n keys to en.json and th.json

### Phase 2: Core Implementation (5 Features)
Build the 5 feature UIs in parallel:
1. SPD Management page (view/edit team time, OT, schedule, leave docs)
2. HRBP Reports page (analytics dashboard)
3. Geofencing Config component (zone CRUD within Settings)
4. Workflow Config component (approval step configuration within Settings)
5. Cancel-approved-leave enhancement in leave history

### Phase 3: Integration & Polish
- Wire up navigation (sidebar, header menus)
- Create route pages
- RBAC-gate all new pages
- Visual QA across desktop/mobile
- Verify i18n completeness

## Team Orchestration

- You operate as the team lead and orchestrate the team to execute the plan.
- You're responsible for deploying the right team members with the right context to execute the plan.
- IMPORTANT: You NEVER operate directly on the codebase. You use `Task` and `Task*` tools to deploy team members to do the building, validating, testing, deploying, and other tasks.

### Team Members

- Builder
  - Name: builder-foundation
  - Role: Create all new hooks, update RBAC, add i18n keys
  - Agent Type: builder
  - Resume: true

- Builder
  - Name: builder-spd
  - Role: Build SPD Management page with team time/OT/schedule/leave-doc tabs
  - Agent Type: builder
  - Resume: true

- Builder
  - Name: builder-hrbp
  - Role: Build HRBP Reports dashboard page
  - Agent Type: builder
  - Resume: true

- Builder
  - Name: builder-admin
  - Role: Build Geofencing Config and Workflow Config components in Settings
  - Agent Type: builder
  - Resume: true

- Builder
  - Name: builder-leave-enhancement
  - Role: Enhance leave cancel for approved items, wire 2-step approval
  - Agent Type: builder
  - Resume: true

- Builder
  - Name: builder-integration
  - Role: Wire up routes, navigation, RBAC gates, final polish
  - Agent Type: builder
  - Resume: true

- Validator
  - Name: validator-final
  - Role: Validate all features work, TypeScript compiles, i18n complete, RBAC correct
  - Agent Type: validator
  - Resume: false

## Step by Step Tasks

### 1. Foundation - RBAC & i18n Keys
- **Task ID**: foundation-rbac-i18n
- **Depends On**: none
- **Assigned To**: builder-foundation
- **Agent Type**: builder
- **Parallel**: true (can start immediately)
- Update `src/frontend/src/lib/rbac.ts`:
  - Add `'spd-management': ['manager', 'hr_admin', 'hr_manager']`
  - Add `'hrbp-reports': ['hr_admin', 'hr_manager']`
  - Add `'geofencing-config': ['hr_admin', 'hr_manager']`
  - Add `'workflow-config': ['hr_admin', 'hr_manager']`
- Add i18n keys to `messages/en.json` under new sections: `spd`, `hrbpReports`, `geofencing`, `workflowConfig`
- Add Thai translations to `messages/th.json` for all new keys
- Key groups needed:
  - `spd.title`, `spd.subtitle`, `spd.tabs.*` (timeRecords, otRecords, schedules, leaveDocuments)
  - `spd.columns.*` (employee, date, clockIn, clockOut, workHours, otHours, status, actions)
  - `spd.actions.*` (edit, save, cancel, viewDocument)
  - `hrbpReports.title`, `hrbpReports.subtitle`, `hrbpReports.tabs.*` (attendance, leave, overtime, summary)
  - `hrbpReports.metrics.*` (avgAttendance, lateRate, absentRate, leaveUtilization, otHours, headcount)
  - `geofencing.title`, `geofencing.zones`, `geofencing.addZone`, `geofencing.radius`, `geofencing.latitude`, `geofencing.longitude`, `geofencing.employees`
  - `workflowConfig.title`, `workflowConfig.templates`, `workflowConfig.steps`, `workflowConfig.addStep`, `workflowConfig.approverRole`, `workflowConfig.condition`

### 2. Foundation - New Hooks
- **Task ID**: foundation-hooks
- **Depends On**: none
- **Assigned To**: builder-foundation
- **Agent Type**: builder
- **Parallel**: true (can start with task 1)
- Create `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-spd.ts`:
  - Interface: `TeamMember { id, name, photo, position, department }`
  - Interface: `TeamTimeRecord { id, employeeId, employeeName, date, shift, clockIn, clockOut, workHours, otHours, status, isEdited }`
  - Interface: `TeamOTRecord { id, employeeId, employeeName, date, startTime, endTime, hours, type, status, amount }`
  - Interface: `TeamSchedule { id, employeeId, employeeName, weekSchedule: ShiftDay[] }`
  - Interface: `LeaveDocument { id, employeeId, employeeName, leaveType, startDate, endDate, days, status, hasDocument, documentUrl }`
  - Mock data: 5-8 team members with realistic Thai names, 14 days of time records, 5 OT records, schedules, 4 leave documents
  - Functions: `updateTimeRecord`, `updateOTRecord`, `updateSchedule`, `viewLeaveDocument`
  - Filters: by employee, date range, status
- Create `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-hrbp-reports.ts`:
  - Interface: `AttendanceReport { period, department, headcount, avgAttendanceRate, lateRate, absentRate, totalWorkHours, totalOTHours }`
  - Interface: `LeaveReport { period, department, leaveType, totalDays, employeeCount, utilizationRate }`
  - Interface: `OvertimeReport { period, department, totalHours, totalAmount, employeeCount, avgHoursPerPerson }`
  - Mock data: Monthly reports for 3 departments across 3 months
  - Summary metrics: company-wide attendance rate, leave utilization, OT cost trend
- Create `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-geofencing.ts`:
  - Interface: `GeofenceZone { id, name, latitude, longitude, radius, address, isActive, assignedEmployeeCount }`
  - Mock data: 3 zones (HQ Bangkok, Branch Chiang Mai, Branch Phuket) with realistic coordinates
  - Functions: `addZone`, `updateZone`, `deleteZone`, `toggleZoneActive`
- Create `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-workflow-config.ts`:
  - Interface: `WorkflowTemplate { id, name, type: WorkflowType, isActive, steps: WorkflowStepTemplate[] }`
  - Interface: `WorkflowStepTemplate { step, approverRole: Role | 'direct_manager' | 'hrbp', condition?, isRequired }`
  - Mock templates: leave (2-step: manager -> HR), leave_with_document (2-step: manager -> HRBP), overtime (2-step: manager -> HR), time_correction (1-step: manager), transfer (3-step)
  - Functions: `updateTemplate`, `addStep`, `removeStep`, `toggleTemplateActive`
- Extend `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-workflows.ts`:
  - Add `'time_correction'` to `WorkflowType` union
  - Add mock time_correction workflow item to MOCK_WORKFLOWS

### 3. SPD Management Page
- **Task ID**: build-spd-page
- **Depends On**: foundation-hooks
- **Assigned To**: builder-spd
- **Agent Type**: builder
- **Parallel**: true (can run alongside tasks 4, 5, 6)
- Create `src/frontend/src/components/spd/spd-management-page.tsx`:
  - Page header: "Team Data Management" with subtitle
  - 4 Tabs: Time Records, OT Records, Schedules, Leave Documents
  - **Time Records tab**:
    - Date range filter + employee dropdown
    - Table: Employee | Date | Shift | Clock In | Clock Out | Work Hours | OT | Status | Actions
    - Inline edit: click edit icon -> fields become editable inputs -> save/cancel
    - Color-code late clock-ins (red), early departures (amber)
    - Responsive: card list on mobile, table on desktop
  - **OT Records tab**:
    - Filter by employee, date range, status
    - Table: Employee | Date | Time Range | Hours | Type | Amount | Status | Actions
    - Edit modal for adjusting OT hours
  - **Schedules tab**:
    - Employee list with their weekly schedule grid
    - Edit mode: select shift template per day, save bulk changes
    - Visual: color-coded shift blocks
  - **Leave Documents tab**:
    - List of leave requests that have attached documents
    - View/download document button
    - Status: Pending Review | Reviewed | Requires Resubmission
    - Quick approve/reject action for document verification
  - Follow existing design patterns: use Card, CardHeader, CardContent, Badge, Button, Tabs, FormField, Modal
  - Padding: use `p-5 sm:p-6 lg:p-8` pattern for CardContent

### 4. HRBP Reports Page
- **Task ID**: build-hrbp-page
- **Depends On**: foundation-hooks

  - Department filter dropdown

  - **Attendance tab**:
    - Monthly trend display (text-based, no charts - just table/card format)
    - Table: Month | Headcount | Attendance Rate | Late Rate | Absent Rate | Total Work Hours
    - Department comparison cards
  - **Leave tab**:

  - **Overtime tab**:

### 5. Admin Geofencing Configuration

### 6. Admin Workflow Configuration

- Create `src/frontend/src/components/settings/workflow-config.tsx`:

  - Template list: one card per workflow type (Leave, Leave with Document, Overtime, Time Correction, Transfer, Resignation)

### 7. Leave Cancel Enhancement

- Modify `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-leave.ts`:

### 8. Route & Navigation Integration

  - Under "Organization" section: add "Team Data (SPD)" link to `/spd-management`
  - Under "Organization" section: add "HRBP Reports" link to `/hrbp-reports`
- Verify RBAC gates work correctly:
  - SPD page: accessible by manager, hr_admin, hr_manager
  - HRBP Reports: accessible by hr_admin, hr_manager
  - Settings tabs (Geofencing, Workflow): accessible by hr_admin, hr_manager (already gated by Settings page)

### 9. Final Validation
- **Task ID**: validate-all
- **Depends On**: integration-routes-nav
- **Assigned To**: validator-final
- **Agent Type**: validator
- **Parallel**: false
- Run TypeScript compilation: `cd src/frontend && npx tsc --noEmit`
- Verify all new pages load without errors at:
  - `http://localhost:3002/en/spd-management`
  - `http://localhost:3002/en/hrbp-reports`

- Verify RBAC: pages should show "Access Denied" for unauthorized roles

- Check responsive design: desktop (1920px) and mobile (375px) viewports
- Verify no hardcoded `p-3`, `p-4`, or `p-6` without responsive variants on any new CardContent

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SPD Management page renders with 4 tabs (Time Records, OT Records, Schedules, Leave Documents)

- [ ] HRBP Reports shows summary metric cards with department filter
- [ ] Settings page has Geofencing tab with zone CRUD (add, edit, delete, toggle)
- [ ] Settings page has Workflow Config tab showing all workflow templates
- [ ] Workflow Config allows editing step order and approver roles
- [ ] "Leave with Document" template shows 2-step: Direct Manager -> HRBP
- [ ] Leave history shows "Request Cancellation" button for approved future leaves
- [ ] Cancel approved leave creates a workflow for approval
- [ ] All new pages have proper RBAC gating
- [ ] All new text has both English and Thai translations
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Responsive design works on desktop and mobile
- [ ] All CardContent uses `p-5 sm:p-6 lg:p-8` pattern (no hardcoded single values)

## Validation Commands
Execute these commands to validate the task is complete:

- `cd /Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` - Verify TypeScript compilation
- `grep -rn 'CardContent className="p-[2-6]"' src/frontend/src/components/` - Check for padding violations (should return 0 results)
- `grep -c '"spd\.' messages/en.json` - Verify SPD i18n keys exist
- `grep -c '"hrbpReports\.' messages/en.json` - Verify HRBP i18n keys exist
- `grep -c '"geofencing\.' messages/en.json` - Verify geofencing i18n keys exist
- `grep -c '"workflowConfig\.' messages/en.json` - Verify workflow config i18n keys exist

## Notes
- All mock data should use realistic Thai names and Bangkok-area locations
- Follow existing code patterns: IIFE-free, React hooks, `useTranslations()` for all user-facing text
- Use the existing Card component with responsive padding `p-5 sm:p-6 lg:p-8`
- The SPD page is essentially a "team view" version of the employee time page - reuse patterns from time-page.tsx
- HRBP reports are read-only analytics - no data modification, just aggregated views
- Geofencing config stores zone coordinates - actual GPS validation happens on the backend (mock for now)
- Workflow config is a template editor - actual workflow routing logic stays in use-workflows.ts
- The 2-step approval for document leaves is the most complex integration: when a leave has `hasDocument: true`, the workflow template switches from "Leave" to "Leave with Document" which includes HRBP step
- No external libraries needed - all features can be built with existing UI components

tool_input.content:
# Plan: Dark Theme Support

## Task Description
Implement comprehensive dark theme support for the Next.js HRMS frontend (`src/frontend/`). This includes CSS variable-based theming, a theme provider component, theme toggle in Settings and Header, localStorage persistence, system preference detection, and updating all 88+ component files to support dark mode variants. The implementation must preserve the Central Group brand identity (cg-red #C8102E) across both themes.

## Objective
When complete, users will be able to switch between Light, Dark, and System Default themes. The selected theme persists across sessions via localStorage. All pages and components render correctly in both themes with proper contrast, readability, and brand consistency.

## Problem Statement
The app is currently light-only with ~1,700+ hardcoded color classes (`bg-white`, `text-gray-*`, `border-gray-*`) spread across 88 component files. The Zustand UI store already has a `theme` field and i18n keys exist (`settings.theme`, `settings.themeLight`, `settings.themeDark`, `settings.themeSystem`), but none are wired up. Users working in low-light environments have no way to reduce eye strain.

## Solution Approach
Use Tailwind CSS v4's `@theme` block with CSS custom properties and the `dark:` variant (class-based strategy via a `ThemeProvider` component). This avoids touching every hardcoded color class in a single pass -- instead, we:

1. **Phase 1** - Build the CSS foundation: define semantic CSS variables for surface, text, and border colors that swap values in `.dark` context. Update `globals.css` with a `@layer base` dark theme override.
2. **Phase 2** - Create a `ThemeProvider` that reads from Zustand + localStorage + `prefers-color-scheme`, and applies the `dark` class to `<html>`. Add theme toggle UI.
3. **Phase 3** - Update all UI primitives (`card`, `button`, `badge`, `modal`, `tabs`, `form-field`, `skeleton`, `toast`) with `dark:` variants. Since all pages compose these primitives, this gives ~70% coverage.
4. **Phase 4** - Update shared layout components (`sidebar`, `header`, `mobile-menu`, `page-layout`) and the root layout.
5. **Phase 5** - Sweep remaining page components that use inline colors not covered by UI primitives.
6. **Phase 6** - Validation, contrast checking, and E2E testing.

## Relevant Files
Use these files to complete the task:

### Core Infrastructure (modify)
- `src/frontend/src/app/globals.css` - Add dark theme CSS variables and `@layer base` overrides
- `src/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` - Wire ThemeProvider, add `suppressHydrationMismatch` to `<html>`
- `src/frontend/src/stores/ui-store.ts` - Already has `theme` state; add localStorage persistence
- `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-settings.ts` - Already has `theme` field in GeneralSettings
- `src/frontend/src/components/settings/settings-page.tsx` - Add theme toggle to System tab
- `src/frontend/messages/en.json` - Already has theme keys; verify completeness
- `src/frontend/messages/th.json` - Already has theme keys; verify completeness

### New Files

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation - CSS Variables & Theme Infrastructure
Define semantic color tokens in `globals.css` using CSS custom properties that swap in `.dark` context. Create `ThemeProvider` component and `use-theme` hook. Wire into root layout.

**Dark color palette decisions:**
| Token | Light | Dark |
|-------|-------|------|
| `--surface-primary` | `#FFFFFF` | `#1a1a2e` |
| `--surface-secondary` | `#F5F5F5` (cg-light) | `#16213e` |
| `--surface-tertiary` | `#F9FAFB` (gray-50) | `#1e2a47` |
| `--text-primary` | `#333333` (cg-dark) | `#e4e4e7` |
| `--text-secondary` | `#6B7280` (gray-500) | `#a1a1aa` |
| `--text-muted` | `#9CA3AF` (gray-400) | `#71717a` |
| `--border-default` | `#f0f0f0` | `#2d3748` |
| `--border-strong` | `#D1D5DB` (gray-300) | `#4a5568` |
| `cg-red` | `#C8102E` | `#C8102E` (unchanged - brand constant) |
| `cg-red` hover | `#a80d26` | `#e0334a` (lighter for dark bg) |

### Phase 2: Core Implementation - UI Primitives & Layout
Update all 8 UI primitive components and 4 layout components with `dark:` Tailwind variants. This gives the widest coverage since all pages compose from these components.

### Phase 3: Integration & Polish - Page Components, Settings UI, Testing
Sweep all 40+ page components for inline colors. Add theme toggle to Settings and Header. Run E2E validation for visual correctness in both themes.

### 1. CSS Variables & Dark Theme Foundation

- Update `src/frontend/src/app/globals.css`:

### 2. ThemeProvider & use-theme Hook
- **Task ID**: theme-provider
- **Depends On**: css-foundation
- **Assigned To**: builder-css-infra
- **Agent Type**: builder
- **Parallel**: false
- Create `src/frontend/src/hooks/use-theme.ts`:
  - Read theme from `useUIStore`
  - Listen to `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` for `system` mode
  - Compute resolved theme: if `system`, use media query result; otherwise use store value
  - Return `{ theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme }`
- Create `src/frontend/src/components/shared/theme-provider.tsx`:
  - Client component that wraps children
  - On mount: read from `localStorage('theme')` and call `useUIStore.setTheme()`
  - Use `useEffect` to apply/remove `dark` class on `document.documentElement`
  - Persist changes to localStorage when theme changes
  - Include inline `<script>` to prevent FOUC (flash of unstyled content) by checking localStorage before React hydrates
- Update `src/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx`:
  - Add `suppressHydrationMismatch` to `<html>` element
  - Wrap body content with `<ThemeProvider>`
- Update `src/frontend/src/stores/ui-store.ts`:

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Theme can be toggled between Light, Dark, and System Default from both the Settings page (System tab) and a header icon button
- [ ] Selected theme persists across page refreshes and browser sessions via localStorage
- [ ] System Default mode correctly follows the OS `prefers-color-scheme` setting and responds to changes
- [ ] No flash of unstyled content (FOUC) on page load -- theme applied before React hydrates
- [ ] All 8 UI primitive components render correctly in dark mode with proper contrast
- [ ] All 4 layout components (sidebar, header, mobile-menu, page-layout) render correctly in dark mode
- [ ] All 40+ page components have `dark:` variants for inline color classes
- [ ] Central Group brand color (`cg-red` #C8102E) is preserved and visible in both themes
- [ ] Tables, forms, modals, dropdowns, and tooltips all have appropriate dark backgrounds and text colors
- [ ] `npm run build` passes with zero errors
- [ ] No `bg-white` class exists without a corresponding `dark:` variant in any component file under `src/components/`

## Validation Commands
Execute these commands to validate the task is complete:

- `cd src/frontend && npm run build` - Verify the app builds without errors
- `grep -r "bg-white" src/frontend/src/components/ --include="*.tsx" -l` - List files still using `bg-white` (each should also have `dark:bg-*`)
- `grep -rn "bg-white" src/frontend/src/components/ --include="*.tsx" | grep -v "dark:"` - Find `bg-white` without adjacent `dark:` variant (should be zero or near-zero)
- `grep -c "dark:" src/frontend/src/components/ui/*.tsx` - Confirm all UI primitives have dark variants
- `grep -c "dark:" src/frontend/src/components/shared/*.tsx` - Confirm all layout components have dark variants
- `test -f src/frontend/src/components/shared/theme-provider.tsx && echo "EXISTS" || echo "MISSING"` - Verify ThemeProvider exists
- `test -f src/frontend/src/hooks/use-theme.ts && echo "EXISTS" || echo "MISSING"` - Verify use-theme hook exists
- `grep -c "theme" src/frontend/src/app/layout.tsx` - Verify theme wiring in root layout

## Notes
- **Brand color preservation**: `cg-red` (#C8102E) must NOT change between themes. It has sufficient contrast on both light and dark backgrounds. However, hover states may need adjustment (lighter in dark mode for visibility).
- **FOUC prevention**: The ThemeProvider should inject a blocking `<script>` that reads localStorage and applies the `dark` class before React hydrates. This is a common Next.js pattern.
- **Tailwind v4**: This project uses Tailwind v4 with `@theme` blocks (not `tailwind.config.js`). CSS variables defined in `@theme` are automatically available as utilities.
- **No new dependencies needed**: Tailwind's built-in `dark:` variant with class strategy is sufficient. No need for `next-themes` or similar packages.
- **Zustand store**: Already has `theme` state and `setTheme` action -- no store changes needed, only localStorage persistence in the provider.
- **i18n keys**: Theme translation keys already exist in both `en.json` and `th.json`. Verify they include: `settings.theme`, `settings.themeLight`, `settings.themeDark`, `settings.themeSystem`.

tool_input.content:
---
name: feedback-larger-than-normal-text
description: HR users complain text is hard to read — default type a notch larger than normal across the frontend
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

The HR end-users of this app repeatedly complain that text is too small / hard to read ("มองไม่ชัด"). Default to type that runs **a notch larger than the usual web norm** across `src/frontend`, not standard sizing.

**Why:** real audience legibility — not a one-off aesthetic tweak; it's a standing accessibility requirement for this product's users.

**How to apply:** Two levers, applied 2026-05-25:
1. Global base — `html { font-size: 17.5px }` in `src/app/globals.css` (up from the 16px default). This scales all rem-based Tailwind `text-*` utilities app-wide, so most page content grows automatically. Keep new page content on Tailwind text utilities so it inherits this.
2. px-based chrome (sidebar/topbar/custom CSS) does NOT scale with the root bump, so size those up explicitly. Sidebar (`bp-rail`/`bp-panel`) ended at: rail label 12.5px, panel item 15.5px, panel title 18.5px, tenant 11.5px, footer name 15.5px.

When building/reviewing UI here, err on the larger side for font-size and prefer rem/Tailwind text utilities so the global lever reaches it. If the user says "still too small," push sizes up further — they have a high legibility bar. Verified the 17.5px bump causes no horizontal overflow on /home.

---
name: reference-hrms-blueprint-design-source
description: Canonical shell/IA/home design lives in the separate HRMS_Blueprint project, not in this repo
metadata:
  type: reference
---

The design source of truth for the HRMS shell (sidebar IA, topbar, home, module screens) is a **separate project**: `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/HRMS_Blueprint` (a static JSX/HTML prototype served locally on ports 8010/8011; also opened as the `HRMS Modules.html` artifact on claude.ai/design). When the user says "blueprint" / "ตาม blueprint" / "HRMS Modules", they mean this project.

Key files there: `hrms-shell.jsx` (MODULES IA array + Sidebar/Topbar/App), `hrms-shell.css`, `HRMS Modules.html`, `humi-prototype.jsx`, `mod-*.jsx` module screens.

tool_input.description: Draft consensus plan for 7 reqs
tool_input.prompt:
Create a detailed implementation PLAN (not code) for a 7-part UI feature set in the Next.js HRMS frontend at `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend`. This is the FIRST pass of an OMC ralplan consensus loop — your plan will be reviewed by Architect then Critic, so make it concrete and testable.

## CRITICAL PROJECT CONTEXT
- **Phase = UI Mockup for HR sign-off.** Backend is OUT OF SCOPE. No real API/persistence/auth wiring. In scope: clickable flows, modals open, tables render, filters work, navigation, visible state transitions, realistic mock/registry data, Humi design conformance, bilingual TH/EN parity.
- **Humi design system is canonical.** Cream canvas + navy ink, teal/accent for primary/active, indigo for info, pumpkin `--color-danger` for danger (NEVER red). Use Humi primitives (Card, Button, DataTable, Modal, FormField, EmptyState) + token utilities (bg-canvas, bg-surface, text-ink, text-ink-muted, border-hairline, rounded-[var(--radius-md)]). NO hardcoded hex unless a design token, NO Tailwind red.
- **HR users find text too small** → default a notch larger.
- Branch: `feat/sidebar-ia-restructure` (work in progress, 34 uncommitted files).
- Before any PR, an existing **Linear** ticket (STA-* prefix) should be validated. In your plan, include a step to search Linear for tickets matching each requirement (roster/shifts, acting-as/impersonation ribbon, home quick-actions, persona/proxy switch, approvals queue, topbar inbox/noti) and cite IDs; if none exists, say so explicitly.

## THE 7 REQUIREMENTS (with verified current-state findings)

### Req 1 — Port "Roster & Shifts" page
- Reference: `mod-roster-v2.jsx` in the HRMS App prototype (fetched to `/tmp/hrms_app_ref.html`). It's a **Gantt timeline roster**: employee rows × 24-hour columns, absolutely-positioned shift cells with type variants — Manager (`.s-mgr`, indigo soft), Part-time (`.s-pt`, amber #FEF3C7), Night (`.s-night`, dark navy #1d2b48), Regular (default, teal accent-soft). Plus a coverage strip (ok/gap/over/off colored cells), a Shift Editor side-drawer, a Shift Swap modal, and a Bulk Assign modal.
- Current: NO frontend roster page. Sidebar items `roster` and `swap` (GROUP 2 Team Management) are PLACEHOLDERs pointing to `/manager-dashboard`. There IS a store `src/lib/admin/store/hrbpRoster.ts` (check if reusable for mock data).
- Target: create a real route (propose `/[locale]/roster` or under team-management) with Humi-tokenized gantt grid + the drawer/modals as clickable mockups with mock data. Wire sidebar `roster` (and decide `swap` → Shift Swap modal or its own route).

### Req 2 — "Acting as" login ribbon (wire it in)
- `src/components/humi/shell/LoginAsRibbon.tsx` EXISTS (82 lines) but is **dead code — imported nowhere**. It reads `useAuthStore` (`username`, `originalUser`, `exitPersona()`).
- Blueprint pattern (`HRMS_Blueprint/hrms-shell.jsx`): amber/orange ribbon at very top, ABOVE/beside topbar. Copy: EN "Acting as {Name} · EMP-{id} · SCOPE · Switch back to admin"; TH "กำลังดูในชื่อ {Name} · EMP-{id} · สิทธิ์ · กลับสู่ผู้ดูแลระบบ". User's Image showed orange ribbon "Acting as Somchai K. EMP-00204 SCOPE · A + B … Switch back to admin".
- Current Topbar still renders deprecated `<ActingBadge/>` (line 186). Plan: wire LoginAsRibbon into AppShell as a top ribbon (above Topbar), align copy/colors to blueprint (amber, show EMP id + SCOPE/access tiers), and REMOVE the now-duplicate ActingBadge from Topbar.

### Req 3 — Home: 12 quick-access tiles, NO calendar
- Home: `src/app/[locale]/home/page.tsx` (662 lines). Has a `<QuickActionsTile>` (dynamic, from admin config bus / DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS) and a **calendar/birthdays widget in Row 3 (lines ~500+)**.
- Target: a quick-action section with exactly **12 quick-access tiles** (decide source: extend DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS to 12, role-gated, bilingual labels + icons), and **REMOVE the calendar widget**. Reflow the layout so removing the calendar doesn't leave a hole. Blueprint quick-tiles use `.quick-grid`/`.quick-tile`/`.quick-ico` with colored icon badges (teal/indigo/amber/coral soft).

### Req 4 — Proxy switch per blueprint
- `src/components/humi/shell/PersonaSwitcher.tsx` exists (demo-only, `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE` gated), uses `useAuthStore.switchPersona()/exitPersona()`, lists DEMO_USERS.
- Blueprint `PersonaSwitcher` is a modal: eyebrow "RBAC · 4 tiers", title "Switch persona", rows w/ avatar + name + "{role} · {empId}" + access-tier chips A/B/C/D, footer "RBAC enforced...". Align the existing component to this blueprint shape (RBAC tier chips, empId, copy) without breaking the store wiring.

### Req 5 — Recheck duplicate features
- Known dups to resolve/flag: (a) ActingBadge vs LoginAsRibbon (consolidate → LoginAsRibbon, see Req2); (b) TodoBell vs NotificationBell — both kept but clarify roles (inbox vs noti, see Req6); (c) sidebar GROUP2 `inbox`→/quick-approve AND `approvals`→/quick-approve = duplicate destination; (d) many sidebar PLACEHOLDERs collide (roster/swap/probation all →/manager-dashboard; multiple HR leaves →/admin/benefits, /admin/hire). Plan an explicit audit pass over `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` MODULES array: list every duplicate destination, recommend dedupe/relabel/repoint, and remove dead legacy blocks (there's a SIDEBAR_LEGACY comment block ~lines 383-410). Output a concrete dedupe decision table.

### Req 6 — Inbox + Notification in topbar
- `src/components/humi/shell/Topbar.tsx`: NotificationBell IS rendered (line 185); **TodoBell (inbox) is NOT** rendered (dead code). TodoBell = task inbox (ListTodo icon, footer →/ess/workflows); NotificationBell = system noti (Bell icon, footer →/admin/system/notifications).
- Target: render BOTH in topbar — TodoBell (inbox/envelope icon, matching user's Image which shows an envelope-with-dot + a bell) then NotificationBell, with correct badge counts and TH/EN. Confirm icon choice matches the blueprint (envelope for inbox).

### Req 7 — Simplify the Approvals "approve box" (NEW, from Image #4)
- Reference Image #4 shows the Team Management → Approvals queue as a CLEAN TABLE: breadcrumb "HUMI · TEAM MANAGEMENT · APPROVALS", title "Approvals queue", subtitle "7 items pending your decision", a filter segmented control "All · 7 / Pending · 7 / Approved · 0 / Rejected · 0", then a table with columns **REF · EMPLOYEE (avatar+name) · TYPE · FILED (date·time) · DETAIL · STATUS (Pending/Review badge)** and inline **Approve / Reject / View** buttons per row.
- Current approvals live under `/[locale]/quick-approve` (and bulk + [id] detail). The user wants the approve box SIMPLIFIED to this table form. Plan: locate the current quick-approve page, compare to Image #4, and define the simplified table (Humi DataTable, segmented filter, inline actions, mock rows like REQ-0204 Leave swap, REQ-0203 Late clock-in, etc.). Keep detail page at `/quick-approve/[id]`. Respect the "unified approval inbox" rule (approvals stay in the /quick-approve umbrella, not standalone per-feature pages).

## DELIVERABLE FORMAT
Produce:
1. A per-requirement plan: files to create/modify (exact paths), what changes, mock data needed, TH/EN keys to add (`src/messages`), and how each becomes a clickable mockup.
2. **Testable acceptance criteria** per requirement (Vitest unit/component tests in co-located `__tests__/` + a Playwright smoke note — recall the project's canonical smoke + screenshot habit at :3000).
3. **Concrete verification steps** (commands: `npm test -- --run <pattern>`, `npm run build`, Playwright smoke).
4. Sequencing/dependencies across the 7 (e.g., Req5 dedupe should land before/with Req1 sidebar wiring; Req2 ribbon interacts with Req4 proxy).
5. A multi-PR breakdown recommendation (this team uses one-ralplan-then-per-PR cadence).
6. **RALPLAN-DR summary** at the end: Principles (3-5), Decision Drivers (top 3), Viable Options (≥2 with bounded pros/cons; if one option, justify why alternatives are invalidated).

Be specific and cite real file paths/line numbers. Do not write production code — this is a plan. You may read files to ground the plan.

# Plan — 7-Part Shell/IA UI Feature Set (Humi Next.js Mockup)

> **Mode:** ralplan consensus, PASS 1 (Planner → Architect → Critic). SHORT mode.
> **Branch context:** `feat/sidebar-ia-restructure` (34 uncommitted shell files in flight).
> **Phase guardrail:** UI Mockup for HR sign-off. Backend OUT OF SCOPE. Clickable flows, mock/registry data, Humi tokens, TH/EN parity only.
> **Frontend root:** `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend`
> **i18n:** `messages/th.json` + `messages/en.json` (frontend root, NOT `src/messages`). `useTranslations('ns')`. `src/lib/__tests__/i18n-completeness.test.ts` enforces TH↔EN key parity — every new key MUST exist in both files.

---

## Context (verified current state)

| Area | File (verified) | Current state |
|---|---|---|
| Shell composition | `src/components/humi/shell/AppShell.tsx` | Renders `<Sidebar/>` + `humi-main` (`<Topbar/>` + page). NO ribbon slot. |
| Topbar | `src/components/humi/shell/Topbar.tsx` | Renders `<NotificationBell/>` (L185) + `<ActingBadge/>` (L186, deprecated) + `<PersonaSwitcher/>` (L187). TodoBell NOT rendered. |
| Inbox bell | `src/components/humi/TodoBell.tsx` | EXISTS, complete (`ListTodo` icon, footer→`/ess/workflows`, `MOCK_TODOS`). Imported NOWHERE. |
| Noti bell | `src/components/humi/NotificationBell.tsx` | Rendered. `Bell` icon, footer→`/admin/system/notifications`, `MOCK_IN_APP_NOTIFICATIONS`. |
| Acting ribbon | `src/components/humi/shell/LoginAsRibbon.tsx` | EXISTS (82 lines), dead code. Reads `username/email/originalUser/exitPersona`, `PERSONA_BADGE`. NOT amber, NOT showing EMP id/SCOPE. |
| Acting badge (dup) | `src/components/humi/ActingBadge.tsx` | 39 lines, rendered in Topbar. Duplicate of LoginAsRibbon's role. |
| Proxy switch | `src/components/humi/shell/PersonaSwitcher.tsx` | Dropdown (not modal). Renders always; `switchPersona` demo-gated (`NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE`, auth-store L68). Uses `DEMO_USERS`/`PERSONA_ORDER`/`PERSONA_BADGE`. No RBAC tier chips, no empId, no modal eyebrow. |
| Home | `src/app/[locale]/home/page.tsx` (662 lines) | Row 1.5 `<QuickActionsTile>`; Row 3 (L501-585) has calendar grid + `HUMI_CAL_EVENTS`. Calendar i18n keys `humiHero.calendar*`, `prevMonth`, `nextMonth`. |
| Quick actions | `src/components/humi/QuickActionsTile.tsx` | `DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS` = 5 tiles, 4-col grid, single accent badge color, TH-only labels. `MANAGER_ACTIONS` = 5 tiles. |
| Sidebar | `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` | Blueprint rail+panel. `MODULES` array. Dup destinations: `inbox`+`approvals`→`/quick-approve`; `roster`+`swap`+`probation`→`/manager-dashboard`; multiple HR leaves→`/admin/benefits`,`/admin/hire`. `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` block L383-438. |
| Approvals | `src/app/[locale]/quick-approve/page.tsx` → `src/components/manager/quick-approve-page.tsx` (861 lines) | Heavy workspace: 2 info cards, SmartTabs, filter strip, DataTable w/ select+chain+urgency cols, bulk toolbar, delegation modal. Detail at `/quick-approve/[id]`. |
| Roster | (none) | NO roster page. Ref `mod-roster-v2` in `/tmp/hrms_app_ref.html`. `.shift` absolute cells; `.s-mgr`=accent-alt-soft, `.s-pt`=#FEF3C7 (HARDCODED amber), `.s-night`=#1d2b48 navy; `.cov-strip` 24-col grid; `.cov-cell` 18px. `src/lib/admin/store/hrbpRoster.ts` = HRBP people list (NOT shift data — reusable only for employee-row labels). |

### Linear search results (cite per requirement)

Searched via `mcp__linear__list_issues` (team Stark-xix / project HR). The MCP `/sse` transport intermittently rejects (deprecation) — results below are from the queries that succeeded; remaining gaps flagged for Architect to confirm against `https://mcp.linear.app/mcp`.

| Req | Matching ticket(s) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 1 Roster & Shifts | **NONE FOUND** | No roster/shift/Gantt ticket. New surface — create a ticket before PR, or confirm scope is covered by an existing UI-mockup umbrella. |
| 2 Acting ribbon | **STA-55** "[Audit][EC] Surface Acting assignment in employee lifecycle actions" (Backlog) | STA-55 is about the `acting` *assignment* action card on Employee Detail — adjacent, not the shell ribbon. Ribbon wiring has NO dedicated ticket. Flag. |
| 3 Home 12 tiles / no calendar | **NONE FOUND** | No home-tile/calendar ticket. New scope. |
| 4 Proxy switch | **NONE FOUND** directly; STA-68 (persona ownership scope, Done) adjacent | PersonaSwitcher blueprint-align has no ticket. Flag. |
| 5 Dedupe audit | **NONE FOUND** directly; STA-58/STA-66 are sidebar-route audits (Backlog) | STA-58 (payroll nav), STA-66 (time nav) touch the same `Sidebar.tsx` MODULES. Coordinate to avoid collisions. |
| 6 Inbox+Noti topbar | **NONE FOUND** | New scope. |
| 7 Simplify approve box | **STA-51** (unified inbox, **Duplicate/Canceled**), **STA-46** (approval taxonomy, In Review), **STA-78** "[BE] Claim Approve" (Done), **STA-79** "[BE] Claim Approval Details" (Done) | STA-78/79 already touched `/quick-approve` + `/quick-approve/WF-2026-004` detail with Linear screenshots. Image #4 simplification likely a follow-up to STA-78. STA-51's directive ("กล่องอนุมัติควรมีแค่กล่องเดียว") = the "unified approval inbox" memory rule. Cite STA-78/STA-51 in PR. |

**Action for executor:** before opening each PR, re-run the Linear search against the live endpoint, cite the IDs above, and for Reqs 1/3/4/6 create UI-mockup tickets (or attach to an umbrella) since none exist. State explicitly in the PR body when no ticket exists.

---

## Guardrails

**Must have**
- All new surfaces use Humi primitives (`Card`, `Button`, `DataTable`, `Modal`, `FormField`, `EmptyState`) from `@/components/humi` and token utilities (`bg-canvas`, `bg-surface`, `text-ink`, `text-ink-muted`, `border-hairline`, `rounded-[var(--radius-md)]`).
- Danger = `--color-danger` (pumpkin) / `bg-danger` / `text-danger`. NEVER Tailwind `red-*`.
- TH + EN parity for every user-facing string; keys added to BOTH `messages/th.json` and `messages/en.json` (i18n-completeness test will fail otherwise).
- Text a notch larger than default (HR readability rule) — prefer existing `text-sm`→`text-base` bumps on body copy, no sub-13px chrome.
- Co-located Vitest tests in `__tests__/` beside each touched component; Playwright smoke at `:3000` with screenshots posted to the linked ticket/PR.

**Must NOT have**
- No real API/persistence/auth wiring. No POST/PUT handlers. Local state + mock registries only.
- No hardcoded hex (the ref's `#FEF3C7`, `#1d2b48`, `#B5BBF1` MUST map to tokens: see Req 1 token table).
- No Tailwind red. No new global CSS unless reusing/extending the existing `bp-*` / `humi-*` blocks in `src/app/globals.css`.
- No architecture redesign of the auth store or routing. Wire existing pieces.

---

## Sequencing & dependencies

```
PR-1  Req5  Sidebar dedupe audit            ── foundation (decision table; repoints roster/swap/inbox)
       │
PR-2  Req6  Topbar inbox+noti  ─┐           ── independent of PR-1
PR-3  Req2  Acting ribbon       ┼─ shell trio (Req2+Req4+Req6 all touch chrome; land PR-2→PR-3→PR-4 in order to avoid Topbar/AppShell merge churn)
PR-4  Req4  Proxy modal align  ─┘           ── Req2 ribbon + Req4 modal share originalUser/exitPersona; Req4 after Req2 so ribbon copy is settled
       │
PR-5  Req1  Roster & Shifts page            ── depends on PR-1 (sidebar roster/swap repoint) for the entry point
PR-6  Req3  Home 12 tiles + remove calendar ── independent; can run parallel to PR-5
PR-7  Req7  Simplify approvals table        ── independent; respects unified-inbox rule (STA-51)
```

Rationale: **Req5 dedupe lands first** so Req1's sidebar `roster`/`swap` repoint is part of an already-deduped MODULES array (no double edit). Reqs 2/4/6 are batched as the "shell trio" because all three edit `Topbar.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx` — serializing them prevents repeated merge conflicts on the same files. Reqs 1/3/7 are leaf surfaces, independent.

---

## Per-requirement plan

### Req 5 — Sidebar dedupe audit  (PR-1, FIRST)

**Files**
- Modify `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` (`MODULES` array L96-194; `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` block L383-438).
- Add `src/components/humi/shell/__tests__/sidebar-dedupe.test.tsx`.

**Dedupe decision table** (executor produces final; this is the recommendation):

| # | Leaf(s) | Current href | Decision | Target after |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `inbox` (badge 12) + `approvals` | both `/quick-approve` | **Merge** — remove `inbox`, keep `approvals` (relabel "Team Inbox · Approvals" / "กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ"), carry badge 12 onto it | one `/quick-approve` leaf (honors unified-inbox rule + STA-51) |
| 2 | `roster` | `/manager-dashboard` (placeholder) | **Repoint** → `/roster` (created in Req1) | `/roster` |
| 3 | `swap` | `/manager-dashboard` (placeholder) | **Repoint** → `/roster?panel=swap` (opens Shift Swap modal on the roster page; avoids a near-empty standalone route) | `/roster?panel=swap` |
| 4 | `probation` | `/manager-dashboard` (placeholder) | **Keep placeholder** but relabel to make the stub honest, OR repoint to existing `/workflows/probation` family if a list route exists (executor to verify). Out of this PR's hard scope — flag in open-questions. | unchanged / verified |
| 5 | `welfare` + `claims` | both `/admin/benefits` | **Keep both** (distinct concepts) but add `#plans` / `#claims` hash so they land on different tabs (no dead-feeling dup) | `/admin/benefits#plans`, `/admin/benefits#claims` |
| 6 | `lifecycle` + `hire` | both `/admin/hire` | **Keep both**, `lifecycle`→`/admin/hire?view=onboarding` | differentiated |
| 7 | `policy`/`workflows`/`notifs`/`branding` | all `/integrations` | **Collapse rail clutter**: keep `integrations` as the real one; relabel the 3 placeholders with `?section=` deep-links so each is distinguishable | `/integrations?section=...` |
| 8 | `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` L383-438 | comment block | **Audit + prune** entries that the Blueprint MODULES now surface directly (the block's own note says it's stale). Remove dead lines; keep genuine URL-only alt-paths. | trimmed block |

**Mock data:** none (pure IA edit).
**TH/EN keys:** relabels stay inline in `MODULES` (`label`/`labelTh`) — no message-file keys needed.

**Acceptance criteria (Vitest)** — `sidebar-dedupe.test.tsx`:
- AC5.1: No two visible leaves share an identical resolved `href` for any single persona (assert by building the leaf set for each of the 6 personas and checking for duplicate bare hrefs). *Exception list* allowed only for documented hash-differentiated pairs (`#plans` vs `#claims`).
- AC5.2: `roster` leaf resolves to `/roster`; `swap` leaf resolves to `/roster?panel=swap`.
- AC5.3: exactly one leaf resolves to `/quick-approve` (the merged approvals entry) and it carries badge `12`.
- AC5.4: manager persona still unlocks the team group (regression vs existing `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx`).

**Verification:** `npm test -- --run sidebar-dedupe` and `npm test -- --run sf-parity-sidebar` (no regression). `npm run build`.

---

### Req 2 — Acting-as login ribbon  (PR-3, shell trio)

**Files**
- Modify `src/components/humi/shell/LoginAsRibbon.tsx` — re-skin to amber/orange blueprint ribbon; show `EMP-{id}` + SCOPE/access tiers; bilingual copy.
- Modify `src/components/humi/shell/AppShell.tsx` — render `<LoginAsRibbon/>` ABOVE `<Topbar/>` inside `humi-main` (new top slot, before L284 `<Topbar.../>`).
- Modify `src/components/humi/shell/Topbar.tsx` — REMOVE `<ActingBadge/>` (L186) + its import (L31).
- Delete-candidate: `src/components/humi/ActingBadge.tsx` (mark dead in Req5 audit; physical delete in this PR once no importer remains — verify via grep).
- Add `src/components/humi/shell/__tests__/login-ribbon.test.tsx`.

**What changes:** Ribbon currently only shows when authenticated with a subtle cream/accent strip. Blueprint wants: amber/orange band, copy `Acting as {Name} · EMP-{id} · {SCOPE} · Switch back to admin`. Pull `EMP-{id}` from the active persona (auth-store `userId`; map via `DEMO_USERS[email].id`). SCOPE/access tiers from `PERSONA_BADGE` (label) — and a tier string (A/B/C/D) computed from roles (helper, see Req4 shared tier map). Only the *impersonating* state (`originalUser !== null`) shows the amber emphasis + "Switch back" button; otherwise keep a quiet non-amber identity strip (or render nothing when not in proxy — executor to confirm with Architect; blueprint shows the amber band specifically during impersonation).

**Color tokens:** amber must use an existing token. Candidates in repo: `--color-warning` / `humi-tag--butter` / `bg-warning-tint`. Use `bg-warning-tint` + `text-warning` + `border-warning` (already used by `DelegationBanner` in quick-approve-page.tsx L267 — reuse that exact pattern for consistency). NO new hex.

**Mock data:** uses live `DEMO_USERS`. No new mock.

**TH/EN keys (`shell.ribbon.*`):**
- `actingAs` — EN "Acting as" / TH "กำลังดูในชื่อ"
- `switchBack` — EN "Switch back to admin" / TH "กลับสู่ผู้ดูแลระบบ"
- `scope` — EN "Scope" / TH "สิทธิ์"
(empId + name interpolated.)

**Clickable mockup:** "Switch back" calls existing `exitPersona()` then routes to `/home` (already wired in LoginAsRibbon L37-40).

**Acceptance criteria (Vitest)** — `login-ribbon.test.tsx`:
- AC2.1: When `originalUser` set, ribbon renders the amber band (`bg-warning-tint`/`border-warning` class present) with text matching `Acting as {name}` (EN) and `กำลังดูในชื่อ {name}` (TH).
- AC2.2: Ribbon shows `EMP-{id}` derived from the active persona.
- AC2.3: "Switch back" button calls `exitPersona` (mock store) and navigates to `/home`.
- AC2.4: When NOT impersonating, ribbon does not render the amber "Switch back" button.
- AC2.5: AppShell renders the ribbon above the Topbar (DOM order assertion).
- AC2.6: Topbar no longer imports/renders `ActingBadge` (grep-style assertion / queryByText absent).

**Verification:** `npm test -- --run login-ribbon`; `grep -rn ActingBadge src/` returns no live importer; `npm run build`; Playwright smoke: login as admin → switch persona → assert amber ribbon visible → click Switch back → screenshot.

---

### Req 6 — Inbox + Notification bells in topbar  (PR-2, shell trio, lands first of trio)

**Files**
- Modify `src/components/humi/shell/Topbar.tsx` — import `TodoBell`; render `<TodoBell/>` then `<NotificationBell/>` (inbox-then-bell order, matching Image which shows envelope+dot then bell). Insert before L185.
- Add `src/components/humi/shell/__tests__/topbar-bells.test.tsx` (or extend an existing Topbar test if present).
- Possibly modify `src/components/humi/TodoBell.tsx` — confirm icon. Image shows an **envelope-with-dot** for inbox. Current TodoBell uses `ListTodo`. Decision: switch the trigger icon to `Mail`/`MailOpen` (lucide envelope) per Image #6, keep the popover content. Keep `ListTodo` for the empty-state glyph or also switch — executor + Architect to confirm against blueprint.

**Badge counts:** TodoBell already computes `pendingCount` from `MOCK_TODOS`; NotificationBell computes `unreadCount` from `MOCK_IN_APP_NOTIFICATIONS`. Both use `bg-danger` badge (correct — pumpkin, not red). No change needed to counts.

**Mock data:** existing `src/data/todos/mock.ts` + `src/data/notifications/mock.ts`. Ensure both have ≥1 unread so badges show in the demo.

**TH/EN keys:** TodoBell + NotificationBell are self-localizing via `useParams` locale + inline strings (no message-file keys). If switching to envelope, update `aria-label` strings inline (TH "กล่องข้อความเข้า" / EN "Inbox") — keep inline pattern.

**Acceptance criteria (Vitest)** — `topbar-bells.test.tsx`:
- AC6.1: Topbar renders BOTH a TodoBell trigger (aria-label inbox/to-do) and a NotificationBell trigger (aria-label notifications).
- AC6.2: TodoBell trigger uses an envelope icon (assert lucide `Mail`/`MailOpen` SVG present) per Image #6.
- AC6.3: TodoBell badge shows pending count from `MOCK_TODOS`; NotificationBell badge shows unread count from `MOCK_IN_APP_NOTIFICATIONS`; both badges use `bg-danger`.
- AC6.4: DOM order is TodoBell before NotificationBell.
- AC6.5: TodoBell footer link → `/{locale}/ess/workflows`; NotificationBell footer → `/{locale}/admin/system/notifications`.

**Verification:** `npm test -- --run topbar-bells`; `npm run build`; Playwright smoke: open both popovers, screenshot the two badges.

---

### Req 4 — Proxy switch per blueprint  (PR-4, shell trio, last)

**Files**
- Modify `src/components/humi/shell/PersonaSwitcher.tsx` — convert dropdown → `Modal` (use `@/components/humi` `Modal`). Add eyebrow "RBAC · 4 tiers", title "Switch persona", per-row avatar + name + `{role} · {empId}` + access-tier chips A/B/C/D, footer "RBAC enforced…".
- Add `src/lib/persona-tiers.ts` (shared) — map `Role[]` → access-tier set (A/B/C/D) + a `tierChips(roles)` helper. Reused by Req2 ribbon.
- Modify `src/lib/demo-users.ts` — optionally add a `tier` field per persona OR derive in `persona-tiers.ts` (prefer derive — no schema churn).
- Add `src/components/humi/shell/__tests__/persona-switcher.test.tsx`.

**What changes:** keep store wiring intact (`switchPersona`/`exitPersona`/`landingForDemoUser`, `PERSONA_ORDER`, `DEMO_USERS`, `PERSONA_BADGE`). Only restructure the presentation from dropdown menu to modal with the blueprint anatomy. Trigger button stays in Topbar (unchanged position). `switchPersona` remains `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE`-gated by the store — the modal still renders rows; in non-demo the switch is a no-op guarded by the store (document this; the mockup runs with demo mode on).

**Tier mapping (recommendation, executor to confirm with Architect):**
- A = full admin (`hr_admin`/`hr_manager`), B = HR partner scope (`hrbp`/`spd`), C = manager scope (`manager`), D = self scope (`employee`). A persona shows the chips for the tiers its roles cover.

**Mock data:** uses `DEMO_USERS` (has `id` for empId, `roles` for tiers).

**TH/EN keys (`shell.persona.*`):**
- `eyebrow` — EN "RBAC · 4 tiers" / TH "RBAC · 4 ระดับ"
- `title` — EN "Switch persona" / TH "สลับบทบาท"
- `footer` — EN "RBAC enforced — you only see what each persona may access." / TH "บังคับใช้ RBAC — เห็นเฉพาะสิทธิ์ของแต่ละบทบาท"
- `active` — EN "Active" / TH "ใช้อยู่"

**Acceptance criteria (Vitest)** — `persona-switcher.test.tsx`:
- AC4.1: Clicking the trigger opens a `Modal` (role="dialog") with eyebrow "RBAC · 4 tiers" and title "Switch persona".
- AC4.2: Each persona row shows name, `{role} · {empId}` (empId from `DEMO_USERS[*].id`), and tier chips A–D matching `tierChips(roles)`.
- AC4.3: Footer "RBAC enforced…" present (both locales).
- AC4.4: Selecting a non-active persona calls `switchPersona` with the right identity and routes to `landingForDemoUser(email, locale)`.
- AC4.5: Active persona row is disabled/marked "Active"/"ใช้อยู่".
- AC4.6 (`persona-tiers.test.ts`): `tierChips(['hr_admin'])` → includes A; `tierChips(['employee'])` → only D; etc.

**Verification:** `npm test -- --run persona-switcher persona-tiers`; `npm run build`; Playwright smoke: open modal, screenshot tier chips, switch persona, confirm ribbon (Req2) updates.

---

### Req 1 — Roster & Shifts page  (PR-5, depends on PR-1)

**Files (new)**
- `src/app/[locale]/roster/page.tsx` — route + page shell.
- `src/components/roster/RosterGantt.tsx` — employee rows × 24-hour columns, absolute-positioned shift cells.
- `src/components/roster/CoverageStrip.tsx` — 24-col coverage row (ok/gap/over/off).
- `src/components/roster/ShiftEditorDrawer.tsx` — side-drawer (open on shift click).

## RALPLAN-DR Summary (for Architect → Critic)

### Principles (5)
1. **Mockup-first**: every behavior is clickable + local-state; zero backend wiring.
2. **Humi tokens are law**: no hardcoded hex, no Tailwind red; danger = pumpkin `--color-danger`.
3. **Wire what exists before building new**: LoginAsRibbon, TodoBell, PersonaSwitcher are dead/partial code to activate, not rebuild.
4. **Dedupe before extend**: the sidebar IA is cleaned (Req5) before new entries (Req1) are wired in.
5. **TH/EN parity is a build gate**: i18n-completeness test must stay green.

### Decision Drivers (top 3)
1. **Merge-conflict minimization** — Reqs 2/4/6 all edit `Topbar.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx`; serialize them (shell trio) to avoid repeated rebases.
2. **Entry-point dependency** — Req1 roster needs the Req5 sidebar repoint to be reachable; Req5 must land first.
3. **Honoring the "unified approval inbox" rule + STA-51** — Req7 must simplify *within* the single `/quick-approve` umbrella, not spawn new approval surfaces.

### Viable Options

**Option A (CHOSEN): 7 PRs, dedupe-first, shell-trio batched.**
- Pros: smallest blast radius per PR; matches validated 7-PR/session cadence; isolates the heavy roster build; each PR independently demoable for HR sign-off.
- Cons: shell trio (PR-2/3/4) has ordering coupling; 7 review cycles.

**Option B: 3 PRs — (1) all shell chrome [Req2+4+5+6], (2) roster [Req1], (3) home+approvals [Req3+7].**
- Pros: fewer review cycles; shell edits land atomically (no inter-PR Topbar churn).
- Cons: PR-1 becomes large + hard to review/revert; a regression in one shell req blocks the others; weaker per-feature HR demo granularity.

**Option C: Single PR (all 7).**
- Pros: one merge.
- Cons: violates the team's per-PR cadence + branch-isolation rule; huge review surface; high revert risk; rejected.

**Decision:** Option A. Option C invalidated by the team's established multi-PR workflow (memory: STA-26 cadence) and branch-isolation rule. Option B kept as fallback if review bandwidth is tight — Architect may collapse PR-2/3/4 into one "shell chrome" PR if desired, since they share files anyway.

### Open questions (to persist)
- Req2: does the ribbon render a quiet identity strip when NOT impersonating, or nothing? (Blueprint shows amber only during impersonation.)
- Req5: `probation` placeholder — repoint to a real `/workflows/probation` list or keep stub?
- Req7: replace the heavy `quick-approve-page.tsx` as the default vs feature-flag the simple table?
- Req3: physically remove calendar i18n keys + `HUMI_CAL_EVENTS` or leave as dead?
- All: Linear has no tickets for Reqs 1/3/4/6 — create UI-mockup tickets vs attach to an umbrella?

tool_input.summary: Revise plan: 7 MUST-FIX from consensus
tool_input.message:
Consensus loop iteration 1: Architect returned REQUEST CHANGES, Critic returned ITERATE. Revise the plan at `.omc/plans/sidebar-shell-7part-ui.md` IN PLACE to address all 7 MUST-FIX items below, then reply with a summary of changes. Keep the strong research/principle articulation — the failures are mechanical, not structural.

MF-1 [CRITICAL] — `bg-warning-tint` does NOT resolve in this Tailwind v4 build. `globals.css` @theme block has `--color-warning-soft: #FEF3C7` but NO `--color-warning-tint`. Compiled CSS confirms warning-tint=0 occurrences. Replace EVERY `bg-warning-tint` in the plan with `bg-warning-soft` (Req2 ribbon AC2.1 class assertion; Req1 token table .s-pt + coverage-gap; Req3 amber tone). `text-warning`/`border-warning` DO resolve, keep those. DROP the citation that says "reuse `quick-approve-page.tsx:267` DelegationBanner pattern" — that banner uses the same dead token and is itself silently broken; do not cite it as working prior-art. Add a one-line executor guardrail: "`-tint` is not a token family here (except the legacy danger-tint alias); verify any token with `grep <token> globals.css` before use."

MF-2 [MAJOR] — Wire-before-build dead link. PR-1/Req5 repoints sidebar `roster`→`/roster` but `/roster` page isn't built until Req1. Fix: move the `roster`/`swap` repoint OUT of PR-1 and INTO the roster PR (so the link goes live the moment the page exists); PR-1 dedupes everything else. Add an AC: "no sidebar leaf resolves to a route lacking a `page.tsx` — enumerate sidebar hrefs, assert each route file exists." Remove the dead-link window; don't mask with a unit test.

MF-3 [MAJOR] — Deleting `ActingBadge.tsx` breaks the build unless you also remove its barrel re-export at `src/components/humi/index.ts:42`. Add `index.ts` to Req2 file list. Add AC2.6: "grep `ActingBadge` across `src/` returns zero references (incl. barrel export)."

MF-4 [MAJOR] — Exit-route inconsistency + locale bug. `LoginAsRibbon.tsx:39` exits to `/${locale}/home`; `PersonaSwitcher.tsx:52` exits to `/${locale}/admin`. Pick ONE canonical destination for "exit impersonation" and make both components use it. Recommend `/${locale}/home`. Rewrite AC2.3 and AC4 to assert the LOCALE-PREFIXED path (`/${locale}/home`, not bare `/home`) and that both controls route to the same place.

MF-5 [MAJOR] — Make "no hardcoded hex / no red" ACs falsifiable. AC1.2, AC3.4, AC7.6 must specify the exact scan: "render the component, query all elements; assert no className matches `/(^|\s)(bg|text|border)-(red|rose|pink)-\d/` and no inline style string matches `/#([0-9a-fA-F]{3,8})\b/`." Scope the scan to the NEW component's rendered output only (the global codebase has legitimate `var(--color-warning-soft, #fffbeb)` inline fallbacks that must not trip it).

MF-6 [MAJOR] — Resolve the 7-PR vs 5-PR fork. The plan states Option A = 7 PRs as CHOSEN but then says "Architect may collapse PR-2/3/4." A plan can't go to execution with PR count undecided. ADOPT the 5-PR shape: collapse the shell-chrome work (Reqs 2/4/6, all editing Topbar.tsx/AppShell.tsx) into ONE `feat/shell-chrome` PR — this directly serves Decision Driver #1 (merge-conflict minimization) and is principle-consistent. Final 5 PRs: (1) Sidebar dedupe [Req5, minus roster repoint], (2) shell-chrome [Req2+4+6], (3) roster gantt + roster/swap repoint [Req1+the MF-2 repoint], (4) home 12-tiles + remove calendar [Req3], (5) approvals table [Req7]. Update the sequencing diagram, the multi-PR table, and AC ownership.

MF-7 — Add a proportional PRE-MORTEM (≥3 scenarios) and confirm each is mitigated in the revised plan. Minimum scenarios: (1) amber ribbon renders with no background because token is dead → mitigated by MF-1; (2) HR clicks Roster in sidebar mid-demo → 404 → mitigated by MF-2; (3) i18n key added to only one of messages/{en,th}.json → build gate fails → mitigated by adding keys to both + running i18n-completeness test per PR.

Also fold in these smaller items:
- AC3.3 "no empty hole" → make objective: "assert Week Recognition card renders in Row 3 right column after calendar removal."
- Req6 AC6.2: DECIDE the inbox icon now (don't leave "executor to confirm"). Recommend `Mail`/`MailOpen` (envelope) per the user's reference image which shows an envelope-with-dot, not a checklist.
- Add an ADR section to the final plan (Decision / Drivers / Alternatives considered / Why chosen / Consequences / Follow-ups) — promote the RALPLAN-DR Options+Decision into a formal ADR.
- Note the codebase-wide dead `bg-warning-tint` (~20 files) as an out-of-scope follow-up cleanup ticket (mirrors the danger-tint TODO at globals.css:61).
- Verify (don't assume) that `--color-accent-alt`/`--color-accent-alt-soft` exist before Req1 relies on them. (They do: globals.css:45-46. State it as verified.)
- `.s-night` maps to `bg-[var(--color-ink)]` — add a contrast AC so navy text isn't on navy background (use a light text token).

Reply when the plan file is updated, summarizing what changed per MF item.
