tool_input.new_string:
**Must NOT have**
- No real API/persistence/auth wiring. No POST/PUT handlers. Local state + mock registries only.
- No hardcoded hex (the ref's `#FEF3C7`, `#1d2b48`, `#B5BBF1` MUST map to tokens: see Req 1 token table).
- No Tailwind red. No new global CSS unless reusing/extending the existing `bp-*` / `humi-*` blocks in `src/app/globals.css`.
- No architecture redesign of the auth store or routing. Wire existing pieces.

**Token guardrail (executor — READ FIRST, MF-1):**
- The amber soft-fill token is **`--color-warning-soft`** (globals.css:58 = `#FEF3C7`). Use **`bg-warning-soft`**. **`bg-warning-tint` does NOT exist** in this Tailwind v4 `@theme` block — it compiles to nothing (0 occurrences in compiled CSS) and silently renders no background. Every place this plan needs amber soft-fill uses `bg-warning-soft`.
- `text-warning` and `border-warning` DO resolve (`--color-warning: #F59E0B`, globals.css:57) — keep those.
- **`-tint` is not a token family here.** The only `*-tint` that resolves is the legacy `--color-danger-tint` alias (globals.css:61, itself slated for removal). **Verify any token with `grep <token> src/app/globals.css` before using it in code.**
- Verified-present tokens this plan relies on (confirmed in globals.css): `--color-accent-alt: #5B6CE0` + `--color-accent-alt-soft: #E1E4FB` (45-46); `--color-warning: #F59E0B` (57); `--color-warning-soft: #FEF3C7` (58); `--color-danger: #FB923C` pumpkin (59); `--color-danger-soft: #FFEDD5` (60); `--color-ink` / `--color-canvas-soft` (shell). `--color-warning-tint` is **absent** — never use.
- **Do NOT cite the `DelegationBanner` (`quick-approve-page.tsx:267`) as working amber prior-art** — it uses the dead `bg-warning-tint` and is itself silently broken.
- **Out-of-scope follow-up cleanup ticket:** ~24 files in `src/` already use the dead `bg-warning-tint` class (incl. that DelegationBanner). File a separate cleanup ticket to refactor them to `bg-warning-soft`, mirroring the existing `danger-tint`→`danger-soft` refactor TODO at globals.css:61. NOT part of these 5 PRs.

## Sequencing & dependencies

```
PR-1  Req5  Sidebar dedupe audit            ── foundation (decision table; repoints roster/swap/inbox)
       │
PR-2  Req6  Topbar inbox+noti  ─┐           ── independent of PR-1
PR-3  Req2  Acting ribbon       ┼─ shell trio (Req2+Req4+Req6 all touch chrome; land PR-2→PR-3→PR-4 in order to avoid Topbar/AppShell merge churn)
PR-4  Req4  Proxy modal align  ─┘           ── Req2 ribbon + Req4 modal share originalUser/exitPersona; Req4 after Req2 so ribbon copy is settled
       │
PR-5  Req1  Roster & Shifts page            ── depends on PR-1 (sidebar roster/swap repoint) for the entry point
PR-6  Req3  Home 12 tiles + remove calendar ── independent; can run parallel to PR-5
PR-7  Req7  Simplify approvals table        ── independent; respects unified-inbox rule (STA-51)
```

Rationale: **Req5 dedupe lands first** so Req1's sidebar `roster`/`swap` repoint is part of an already-deduped MODULES array (no double edit). Reqs 2/4/6 are batched as the "shell trio" because all three edit `Topbar.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx` — serializing them prevents repeated merge conflicts on the same files. Reqs 1/3/7 are leaf surfaces, independent.
tool_input.new_string:
## Sequencing & dependencies (5-PR shape, MF-6)

```
PR-1  Req5         Sidebar dedupe audit (MINUS roster/swap repoint)   ── foundation; dedupes everything except roster/swap
       │
PR-2  Req2+4+6     Shell chrome (acting ribbon + proxy modal + topbar  ── ONE PR: all three edit Topbar.tsx / AppShell.tsx,
                   inbox+noti bells)                                      so they land atomically — serves Decision Driver #1
       │
PR-3  Req1         Roster & Shifts page + roster/swap sidebar repoint  ── repoint lives HERE so the link goes live the moment
                   (the MF-2 repoint moves out of PR-1 into here)         /roster page exists — no dead-link window
PR-4  Req3         Home 12 tiles + remove calendar                     ── independent; parallel-safe with PR-3
PR-5  Req7         Simplify approvals table                            ── independent; respects unified-inbox rule (STA-51)
```

Rationale (revised per MF-6 + MF-2):
- **Req5 dedupe lands first**, but it **does NOT touch `roster`/`swap`** — those leaves keep pointing at the existing `/manager-dashboard` placeholder until PR-3 builds `/roster`. This removes the dead-link window the prior 7-PR plan created (MF-2).
- **Reqs 2/4/6 collapse into ONE `feat/shell-chrome` PR (PR-2).** All three edit `Topbar.tsx` and/or `AppShell.tsx`; landing them together is the direct implementation of Decision Driver #1 (merge-conflict minimization) and removes the unresolved "Architect may collapse" fork. Internal ordering within the PR: bells → ribbon → proxy modal (ribbon copy + proxy exit-route must agree, see MF-4).
- **Req1 (PR-3) carries the `roster`/`swap` sidebar repoint itself** — the leaf is repointed to `/roster` / `/roster?panel=swap` in the same commit that adds `src/app/[locale]/roster/page.tsx`, so the sidebar link never resolves to a non-existent route.
- Reqs 3/7 are leaf surfaces, independent, run after the chrome settles.

**Option A (CHOSEN): 7 PRs, dedupe-first, shell-trio batched.**
- Pros: smallest blast radius per PR; matches validated 7-PR/session cadence; isolates the heavy roster build; each PR independently demoable for HR sign-off.
- Cons: shell trio (PR-2/3/4) has ordering coupling; 7 review cycles.

**Option B: 3 PRs — (1) all shell chrome [Req2+4+5+6], (2) roster [Req1], (3) home+approvals [Req3+7].**
- Pros: fewer review cycles; shell edits land atomically (no inter-PR Topbar churn).
- Cons: PR-1 becomes large + hard to review/revert; a regression in one shell req blocks the others; weaker per-feature HR demo granularity.

**Option C: Single PR (all 7).**
- Pros: one merge.
- Cons: violates the team's per-PR cadence + branch-isolation rule; huge review surface; high revert risk; rejected.

## Pre-mortem (MF-7) — failure scenarios + mitigations

Proportional pre-mortem: assume the work shipped and something broke. Each scenario maps to a concrete mitigation already in the revised plan.

| # | Failure scenario (imagined post-ship) | Root cause | Mitigation in this plan | Owning AC |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Amber acting-ribbon renders with NO background during the HR demo — looks like an unstyled grey strip. | Used `bg-warning-tint`, a class that does not exist in the Tailwind v4 `@theme` and compiles to nothing. | MF-1: plan mandates `bg-warning-soft`; token guardrail forbids `-tint`; AC2.1 asserts `bg-warning-soft` present AND `bg-warning-tint` absent. | AC2.1 |
| 2 | HR clicks "Roster" in the sidebar mid-demo and hits a 404 because the link was repointed before the page existed. | Sidebar `roster`→`/roster` repoint landed in an earlier PR than the page. | MF-2: repoint moved OUT of PR-1 INTO PR-3 (same commit as the page); AC5.2 route-existence gate fails loudly if any leaf points at a missing `page.tsx`; AC1.9 confirms the link is live in the page's own PR. | AC5.2, AC1.9 |
| 3 | A new string ships in EN but not TH (or vice-versa); the build/i18n gate fails or the demo shows a raw key. | A key added to only one of `messages/{en,th}.json`. | Keys added to BOTH files for every new namespace (Reqs 1/2/4/7); `npm test -- --run i18n-completeness` is in the per-PR cross-cutting verification and is its own AC for Req3 (AC3.6). | AC3.6 + cross-cutting i18n gate |
| 4 | Deleting `ActingBadge.tsx` red-builds CI because a barrel export still references it. | `src/components/humi/index.ts:42` re-exports the deleted component. | MF-3: `index.ts` added to Req2 file list; AC2.6 asserts zero `ActingBadge` references incl. the barrel; `npm run build` would fail otherwise (the AC's own proof). | AC2.6 |
| 5 | Two "exit impersonation" controls send the user to different pages (`/home` vs `/admin`), confusing the demo. | LoginAsRibbon exits to `/home`, PersonaSwitcher exits to `/admin`. | MF-4: PersonaSwitcher exit changed to `/${locale}/home`; AC2.3 + AC4.7 both assert the locale-prefixed `/${locale}/home` and that PersonaSwitcher no longer routes to `/admin`. | AC2.3, AC4.7 |
| 6 | Night-shift cells are unreadable (navy text on navy background). | `.s-night` mapped to navy bg without flipping the text token. | Contrast smaller-item: token table specifies `text-[var(--color-canvas-soft)]`; AC1.8 asserts light text token present and navy ink token absent on the night cell. | AC1.8 |

All six scenarios are mitigated by an explicit, falsifiable AC in the revised plan.

---

## ADR — Architecture Decision Record

**Title:** Sequence the 7-part Humi shell/IA mockup as 5 PRs, dedupe-first, with the shell-chrome reqs collapsed into one PR.

**Status:** Accepted (ralplan consensus, iteration 2).

**Decision:** Deliver the 7 requirements as **5 sequential PRs**: (1) sidebar dedupe minus roster/swap, (2) shell-chrome = acting ribbon + proxy modal + topbar inbox/noti bells [Reqs 2+4+6], (3) roster page + roster/swap sidebar repoint [Req1], (4) home 12 tiles + remove calendar [Req3], (5) simplified approvals table [Req7]. Build on Humi tokens only (amber = `bg-warning-soft`, danger = pumpkin `--color-danger`), with one canonical proxy-exit destination (`/${locale}/home`), and a route-existence gate that forbids any sidebar leaf pointing at a missing page.

**Drivers (top 3):**
1. Merge-conflict minimization — Reqs 2/4/6 share `Topbar.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx`.
2. Entry-point dependency + no-dead-link — the roster sidebar link must not precede the roster page.
3. Honor the "unified approval inbox" rule + STA-51 — Req7 simplifies within the single `/quick-approve` umbrella.

**Alternatives considered:**
- **7 PRs (one per Req).** Rejected: re-introduces two guaranteed rebases on the shared shell files, fighting Driver #1.
- **Single PR (all 7).** Rejected: violates the team's per-PR cadence + branch-isolation rule; high review/revert risk.
- **Repoint roster in PR-1 (original plan).** Rejected: creates a dead-link window between the repoint PR and the roster-page PR (pre-mortem scenario #2).

**Why chosen:** 5 PRs is the smallest count that keeps the only cross-req-coupled files (the shell) in a single atomic change while preserving per-feature demoability for the three independent leaf surfaces (roster, home, approvals) — and it eliminates the dead-link window by co-locating the roster repoint with the roster page.

**Consequences:**
- Positive: zero inter-PR shell rebases; no dead sidebar links at any commit; each PR independently demoable to HR; falsifiable token/route/locale ACs make the mechanical failure modes test-caught, not demo-caught.
- Negative / accepted cost: PR-2 bundles three shell changes (larger single review); a regression isolated to one shell req is reverted at the granularity of the whole shell-chrome PR. Mitigated by fixed internal commit order (bells → ribbon → proxy) and small per-change diffs.

**Follow-ups (out of scope of these 5 PRs):**
- Cleanup ticket: refactor the ~24 files using the dead `bg-warning-tint` → `bg-warning-soft` (mirrors the `danger-tint`→`danger-soft` TODO at globals.css:61).
- Create Linear UI-mockup tickets for Reqs 1/3/4/6 (none exist); re-run the Linear search against the live `https://mcp.linear.app/mcp` endpoint and cite IDs in each PR.
- Resolve the 5 open questions above (ribbon non-proxy state; probation repoint; approvals replace-vs-flag; calendar key removal; ticket strategy).

tool_input.content:
---
name: feedback_sidebar_menu_simplify_cleanest
description: Sidebar MENU simplification is a distinct concern from feature-dedupe; user wants the cleanest menu where every item maps to a real distinct screen
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

"Simplify the menu / reduce duplicates" (simplify เมนู, ลดของซ้ำซ้อน) is a SEPARATE concern from "recheck duplicate features / dedupe destinations" (the sidebar-shell plan's Req5/PR-1, which only removed duplicate `href` targets + the SIDEBAR_LEGACY block). Do NOT conflate them — the user corrected this twice.

The menu-simplification goal: REDUCE the number of sidebar menu items by cutting PLACEHOLDER leaves and merging overlapping ones, so every visible menu item maps to a real, distinct screen. User's chosen aggressiveness = **cleanest: cut placeholders outright** (not "keep as disabled/coming-soon", not "merge into parent"). On the 4-group/41-leaf Blueprint IA this collapses to ~26 items (Workspace 9→8, Team 7→5, HR 14→8, System 11→5).

**Why:** The ported Blueprint sidebar had ~41 leaves but only ~15 real screens; the rest were PLACEHOLDERs pointing at a handful of shared routes (e.g. 5 System items all →/integrations). HR users want a navigable menu, not a wall of duplicate-destination links.

**How to apply:** When the user introduces a NEW scope item, don't assume it maps to an existing approved-plan requirement — treat it as distinct unless they say otherwise. For menu work specifically: cut dead placeholders, merge true duplicates, keep only items with a distinct real route, default to the cleanest option. This menu work owns `Sidebar.tsx` — keep any parallel build off that file. Relates to [[feedback_unified_approval_inbox]] (inbox+approvals merge to one /quick-approve entry).

tool_input.description: Draft menu-simplification plan (PR-1 replacement)
tool_input.prompt:
Create an implementation PLAN (NOT code — do not edit anything) for a **sidebar MENU simplification** in the Next.js HRMS frontend at `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend`. This is the FIRST pass of an OMC ralplan consensus loop — your plan goes to Architect then Critic, so make it concrete and testable. This work **REPLACES the old PR-1** ("Req5 dedupe") in the existing plan `.omc/plans/sidebar-shell-7part-ui.md` — it supersedes the lighter pointer-dedupe with a full menu reduction.

## CONTEXT (decisions already made with the user — do not relitigate)
- Phase = clickable UI mockup for HR sign-off. NO backend. Humi design system canonical (cream/navy/teal, danger=pumpkin not red, NO hardcoded hex, NO red/rose/pink classes, `bg-warning-soft` NOT the dead `bg-warning-tint`). i18n parity gated by `src/lib/__tests__/i18n-completeness.test.ts`.
- The sidebar IA was ported 1:1 from the HRMS Blueprint into `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` as a `MODULES: ModuleGroup[]` array (4 groups, ~41 leaves) — but ~26 of those leaves are PLACEHOLDERs pointing at a handful of shared routes (true redundancy).
- **User-chosen aggressiveness = CLEANEST: cut placeholder leaves outright** (not "keep disabled", not "coming soon"). Every remaining menu item must map to a real, distinct screen.
- **Integrations is CUT entirely** — there is NO real "connect via web" integration feature; the items that pointed at `/integrations` (Integrations, Policy Builder, Approval Workflows, Branding, Notifications-as-integration) are all removed.
- **Time model: keep BOTH** the project-hours Timesheet (`/time/timesheet`, weekly project-row grid via `useTimesheet`) AND the new manager Roster & Shifts (`/roster`). They are different concepts; LABEL them clearly so they're not confused (menu: Workspace "Time & Attendance"/`เวลาและการเข้างาน` vs Team "Roster & Shifts"/`ตารางกะ`; and relabel the Timesheet page eyebrow/h1 to read as hour-logging, e.g. "บันทึกชั่วโมงงาน", not a shift schedule).

## TARGET MENU (41 → ~25). This is the exact agreed MODULES shape:

**Workspace (My Workspace / พื้นที่ทำงานของฉัน) — 9→8:** home(/home), profile(/profile/me), time(/time, "Time & Attendance"/`เวลาและการเข้างาน`), leaves(/timeoff, badge 3), payslips(/payslip), benefits(__BENEFITS__ sentinel → benefits-hub), documents(/me/documents), announce(/announcements). **CUT `requests`** (folds into leaves/documents).

**Team Management (การจัดการทีม) — 7→5:** approvals(/quick-approve, badge 12, MERGED inbox+approvals → label "กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ" per existing test), roster(/roster, "Roster & Shifts"/`ตารางกะ`), perf(/performance-form), probation(/manager-dashboard/probations), reports(/reports). **CUT `swap`** (Shift Swap is a modal inside the Roster page, not a menu item).

**HR Administration (งานบุคคล) — 14→8:** employees(/admin/employees), orgchart(/org-chart), hire(/admin/hire, merges lifecycle/onboarding), recruit(/recruiting), benefits-admin(/admin/benefits, badge 2, merges welfare+claims), hr-docs(/admin/documents, merges confirm), changes(/admin/change-requests, merges transfer+regular), offboard(/resignation). **CUT/fold:** comp→(under Payroll), assets→Catalog, attendance→Reports, audit→System.

**System Settings (ตั้งค่าระบบ) — 11→4:** roles(/permissions), catalog(/admin/foundation, merges assets), docreview(/admin/documents), audit(/admin/system, merges impers). **CUT entirely:** Integrations, Policy Builder, Approval Workflows, Branding, Notifications-as-integration. (Note: Notifications has a real page at /admin/system/notifications — flag whether to keep it as a 5th System item or leave it accessible only via /admin/system; default = cut for cleanest.)

Preserve in every leaf: the `show: PersonaId[]` role-gates (personas: employee, manager, hradmin, hris, spd, sysadmin; `ALL6` const for everyone), badges ('3' leaves, '12' approvals, '2' benefits-admin), and the `__BENEFITS__` sentinel for benefits.

## KEY FILES & CONSTRAINTS THE PLAN MUST COVER
1. `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` — replace the `MODULES` array (lines ~96–194); also REMOVE the `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` block (lines ~383–438, dead code).
2. **`src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` MUST be updated** — it currently asserts the OLD structure: "surfaces all 9 ESS workspace leaves" listing `ใบคำขอ` (requests, being cut); `hr_admin ... ค่าตอบแทน → /payroll` (comp, being cut); it already expects the merged `กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ` leaf and `queryByText('อนุมัติ')` absent. Enumerate exactly which assertions change (workspace 9→8 drop `ใบคำขอ`; HR leaves; System leaves; roster/probation hrefs). The 4-group structure + locked-group + single-open-accordion + persona-gating behaviours stay.
3. **Roster dead-link**: `/roster` page does NOT exist yet (it's built in PR-3 of the other plan). Decide: either keep `roster` pointing at `/manager-dashboard` (real, no dead-end) until `/roster` lands and flip it in the roster PR, OR co-sequence. The mockup DoD = "no dead ends" — pick the no-dead-link option and state it. Add an AC asserting every sidebar leaf href resolves to an existing `page.tsx` (or documented dynamic/sentinel).
4. **Timesheet vs Roster labeling** (the user's confusion fix): specify the exact label changes — menu labels (already distinct) + the `/time/timesheet/page.tsx` eyebrow/h1 relabel so Timesheet reads as project-hours logging, not a shift schedule.
5. The MODULES use inline `label`/`labelTh` (not i18n message keys), so confirm whether any `messages/{en,th}.json` changes are needed (likely none for the menu; the relabel of the timesheet page may use inline strings too — verify).

## DELIVERABLE
1. Per-area plan: exact file edits, the final MODULES array (you may reproduce/refine the shape above), the test-assertion diff list, the timesheet relabel, the legacy-block removal.
2. **Testable acceptance criteria** (Vitest: the updated sf-parity test must pass; a route-existence assertion; label-distinctness assertions) + verification commands (`npm test -- --run sf-parity-sidebar`, `npm test -- --run i18n-completeness`, `npm run build`, Playwright smoke at :3000 checking the simplified menu renders + no dead links).
3. How this slots in as the **PR-1 replacement** relative to the existing 7-part plan (note dependency: the merged inbox/approvals + roster repoint must stay consistent with PR-3 roster work).
4. **RALPLAN-DR summary**: Principles (3-5), Decision Drivers (top 3), Viable Options (≥2 with bounded pros/cons — e.g. "cut placeholders outright" vs "keep as disabled/coming-soon" vs "merge into parent screens"; justify why cleanest-cut was chosen).

Cite real file paths/line numbers. Read files to ground the plan. Save the plan to `.omc/plans/sidebar-menu-simplify.md`. Do NOT write production code.

tool_input.content:
# Plan — Sidebar MENU Simplification (41 → ~25 leaves)

> **Mode:** ralplan consensus, iteration 1 (Planner → Architect → Critic). SHORT mode.
> **Supersedes:** PR-1 ("Req5 sidebar dedupe") of `.omc/plans/sidebar-shell-7part-ui.md`. This plan REPLACES the lighter pointer-dedupe with a full menu reduction — placeholder leaves are CUT, not repointed.
> **Branch context:** `feat/sidebar-ia-restructure`.
> **Phase guardrail:** UI Mockup for HR sign-off. Backend OUT OF SCOPE. Clickable flows, mock/registry data, Humi tokens, TH/EN parity only. DoD = "no dead ends".
> **Frontend root:** `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend`
> **i18n:** `messages/th.json` + `messages/en.json` (frontend root). `src/lib/__tests__/i18n-completeness.test.ts` enforces EN==TH key parity (exact count equality + identical namespace set).

---

## Context (verified against the live tree, 2026-05-25)

| Fact | Verified value |
|---|---|
| Sidebar source | `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx`. `MODULES: ModuleGroup[]` at **L96–205** (4 groups, 41 leaves). `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` comment block at **L406–463**. |
| Test asserting structure | `src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` (350 lines). Asserts workspace = **9** leaves incl. `ใบคำขอ` (L189), hr leaf `ค่าตอบแทน → /th/payroll` (L271), merged `กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ` (L241), `อนุมัติ` absent (L245). |
| `__BENEFITS__` sentinel | Resolved in `Sidebar.tsx` `leafBareHref` via `benefitsHubRoute(locale)` → `/benefits-hub` (verified `src/lib/benefit-routes.ts`). |
| **`/roster` page** | **ALREADY EXISTS** — `src/app/[locale]/roster/page.tsx` (190 lines: RosterGantt + CoverageStrip + ShiftEditorDrawer + ShiftSwapModal + BulkAssignModal, tokenized, no hex). The 7-part plan's "roster doesn't exist yet" premise is STALE. **No dead-link risk pointing `roster → /roster`.** |
| Probation page | `src/app/[locale]/manager-dashboard/probations/page.tsx` EXISTS (current leaf points at `/workflows/probation`, which also exists — target uses `/manager-dashboard/probations`). |
| Timesheet page | `src/app/[locale]/time/timesheet/page.tsx` — h1 already = `บันทึกเวลางาน`/`Timesheet`, desc = "weekly hours per project". Eyebrow = `เวลางาน`/`Time & Attendance`. Inline strings, NO message-file keys. `/time` landing eyebrow = `เวลาและการเข้างาน`/`Time & Attendance`. |
| All 25 target routes | **EXIST** (verified `find … page.tsx`): home, profile/me, time, timeoff, payslip, me/documents, announcements, quick-approve, roster, performance-form, manager-dashboard/probations, reports, admin/employees, org-chart, admin/hire, recruiting, admin/benefits, admin/documents, admin/change-requests, resignation, permissions, admin/foundation, admin/system, admin/system/notifications. |
| `/requests` route | EXISTS but its menu leaf is being CUT (folds into leaves/documents). Page stays reachable by URL; add a SIDEBAR_LEGACY annotation. |
| `/integrations` route | EXISTS (`src/app/[locale]/integrations/page.tsx`). Its menu leaves are CUT; page stays URL-only (annotate). |

**Net delta vs the 7-part PR-1:** that PR *kept* every placeholder and gave each a `?section=`/`#tab` deep-link to look distinct. This plan *deletes* them. Outcome: 41 → 25 leaves, every leaf maps to a real, distinct `page.tsx`, no deep-link-only "honest stubs."

---

## Guardrails

**Must have**
- Every remaining leaf `href` resolves to an existing `page.tsx` (or the documented `__BENEFITS__` sentinel). Verified above; locked by AC-ROUTE.
- Preserve in every kept leaf: `show: PersonaId[]` gates (`employee|manager|hradmin|hris|spd|sysadmin`, `ALL6`), badges (`leaves`=3, `approvals`=12, `benefits-admin`=2), and the `__BENEFITS__` sentinel on benefits.
- 4-group structure + locked-group + master-detail single-open rail + persona-gating + active-state + footer behaviours UNCHANGED (only the leaf set shrinks).
- TH/EN parity: menu labels are inline `label`/`labelTh` (no message keys) — confirmed no `messages/*.json` change for the menu. Timesheet relabel also inline. i18n-completeness must stay green.
- Humi tokens only (no new CSS needed — leaf rendering is unchanged).

**Must NOT have**
- No new routes, no backend, no API wiring.
- No `?section=`/`#tab` deep-link-only leaves (that was the rejected approach).
- No leaf left pointing at a non-existent page (no dead ends — DoD).
- No change to `Role`/`PersonaId`/`PERSONA_ROLE`/`personaGranted`/`leafVisible` logic — only the `MODULES` data and the dead `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` block.

---

## Target MODULES (the exact agreed shape, 25 leaves)

Reproduced for the executor. Comments annotate every CUT/MERGE. `ALL6` and the `__BENEFITS__` sentinel are unchanged.

```ts
const MODULES: ModuleGroup[] = [
  {
    id: 'workspace', label: 'My Workspace', labelTh: 'พื้นที่ทำงานของฉัน', icon: Users,
    leaves: [
      { id: 'home',      label: 'Home',              labelTh: 'หน้าหลัก',          href: '/home',          show: ALL6 },
      { id: 'profile',   label: 'My Profile',        labelTh: 'โปรไฟล์ของฉัน',     href: '/profile/me',    show: ALL6 },
      { id: 'time',      label: 'Time & Attendance', labelTh: 'เวลาและการเข้างาน', href: '/time',          show: ALL6 }, // labelTh changed ลงเวลา → เวลาและการเข้างาน
      { id: 'leaves',    label: 'Leaves',            labelTh: 'ใบลา',              href: '/timeoff',       badge: '3', show: ALL6 },
      { id: 'payslips',  label: 'Payslips',          labelTh: 'สลิปเงินเดือน',     href: '/payslip',       show: ALL6 },
      { id: 'benefits',  label: 'Benefits',          labelTh: 'สวัสดิการ',         href: '__BENEFITS__',   show: ALL6 },
      { id: 'documents', label: 'Documents',         labelTh: 'เอกสาร',            href: '/me/documents',  show: ALL6 },
      { id: 'announce',  label: 'Announcements',     labelTh: 'ประกาศ',            href: '/announcements', show: ALL6 },
      // CUT: requests (/requests) — folds into leaves/documents. Page stays URL-only.
    ],
  },
  {
    id: 'team', label: 'Team Management', labelTh: 'การจัดการทีม', icon: Network,
    leaves: [
      { id: 'approvals', label: 'Team Inbox · Approvals', labelTh: 'กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ', href: '/quick-approve',              badge: '12', show: ['manager','hradmin','hris','spd','sysadmin'] }, // merged inbox+approvals (unchanged)
      { id: 'roster',    label: 'Roster & Shifts',        labelTh: 'ตารางกะ',           href: '/roster',                     show: ['manager','hradmin','sysadmin'] }, // repointed → real /roster page
      { id: 'perf',      label: 'Team Performance',       labelTh: 'ผลงานทีม',          href: '/performance-form',           show: ['manager','hradmin','sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'probation', label: 'Probation Reviews',      labelTh: 'ทดลองงาน',          href: '/manager-dashboard/probations', show: ['manager','hradmin','sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'reports',   label: 'Reports',                labelTh: 'รายงาน',            href: '/reports',                    show: ['manager','hradmin','hris','spd','sysadmin'] },
      // CUT: swap (Shift Swap) — it is a modal inside /roster (?panel=swap), not a menu item.
    ],
  },
  {
    id: 'hr', label: 'HR Administration', labelTh: 'งานบุคคล', icon: IdCard,
    leaves: [
      { id: 'employees',      label: 'Employees',        labelTh: 'ทะเบียนพนักงาน', href: '/admin/employees',       show: ['hradmin','hris','spd','sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'orgchart',       label: 'Org Chart',        labelTh: 'ผังองค์กร',       href: '/org-chart',             show: ['hradmin','hris','spd','sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'hire',           label: 'Hire & Onboard',   labelTh: 'จ้างงาน',         href: '/admin/hire',            show: ['hradmin','sysadmin'] }, // merges lifecycle/onboarding
      { id: 'recruit',        label: 'Recruitment',      labelTh: 'สรรหา',           href: '/recruiting',            show: ['hradmin','sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'benefits-admin', label: 'Benefits Admin',   labelTh: 'จัดการสวัสดิการ', href: '/admin/benefits',        badge: '2', show: ['hradmin','hris','spd','sysadmin'] }, // merges welfare+claims
      { id: 'hr-docs',        label: 'HR Documents',     labelTh: 'เอกสารบุคคล',     href: '/admin/documents',       show: ['hradmin','sysadmin'] }, // merges confirm
      { id: 'changes',        label: 'Change Requests',  labelTh: 'คำขอเปลี่ยนแปลง', href: '/admin/change-requests', show: ['hradmin','hris','sysadmin'] }, // merges transfer+regular
      { id: 'offboard',       label: 'Offboarding',      labelTh: 'ลาออก',           href: '/resignation',           show: ['hradmin','sysadmin'] },
      // CUT/fold: comp→(Payroll, reached elsewhere), assets→Catalog (System), attendance→Reports, audit→System.
    ],
  },
  {
    id: 'system', label: 'System Settings', labelTh: 'ตั้งค่าระบบ', icon: Settings,
    leaves: [
      { id: 'roles',    label: 'Roles & Permissions', labelTh: 'สิทธิ์ตามบทบาท', href: '/permissions',     show: ['sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'catalog',  label: 'Master Catalog',      labelTh: 'ฐานข้อมูลกลาง',   href: '/admin/foundation', show: ['hris','sysadmin'] }, // merges assets
      { id: 'docreview',label: 'Document Review',     labelTh: 'คิวตรวจเอกสาร',   href: '/admin/documents',  show: ['spd','sysadmin'] },
      { id: 'audit',    label: 'Audit & System',      labelTh: 'บันทึก · ระบบ',   href: '/admin/system',     show: ['hradmin','hris','spd','sysadmin'] }, // merges impers
      // CUT ENTIRELY: Integrations, Policy Builder, Approval Workflows, Branding, Notifications-as-integration.
      // Notifications has a real page (/admin/system/notifications) but is left reachable via /admin/system (default = cut). See Open Question.
    ],
  },
];
```

**Group counts:** Workspace 9→8, Team 7→5, HR 14→8, System 11→4. Total 41 → **25**.

> Duplicate-href note (intentional, allowed): within HR group `hr-docs` and within System group `docreview` BOTH point at `/admin/documents`, but they are in DIFFERENT groups and gated to different personas (`hradmin/sysadmin` vs `spd/sysadmin`). Likewise `audit` (HR-visible) and `catalog`/`audit` interplay. The route-existence AC (every leaf → real page) is the hard gate; cross-group same-route is acceptable because the master-detail rail only ever shows one group's leaves at a time, so a single persona never sees two identical-href leaves in one panel UNLESS they hold both gating personas. Architect: confirm whether `docreview → /admin/documents` should instead point at a distinct review surface (e.g. `/admin/system/security/consent` or keep `/admin/documents`); flagged in Open Questions.

---

## Per-area plan — exact file edits

### Edit 1 — `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` MODULES array (L96–205)

Replace the entire `MODULES` array with the 25-leaf shape above. Mechanical notes for the executor:
- Keep imports (`Users, Network, IdCard, Settings`) — all four group icons still used.
- Keep `ALL6`, `PERSONA_ROLE`, `personaGranted`, `leafVisible`, `leafBareHref`, `leafSuffix`, `RAIL_SHORT`, render JSX — UNCHANGED.
- `time` leaf `labelTh` changes `ลงเวลา` → `เวลาและการเข้างาน` (CONTEXT directive; distinguishes from Team "ตารางกะ").
- `roster` leaf `href` changes `/manager-dashboard` → `/roster` (page now exists).
- `probation` leaf `href` changes `/workflows/probation` → `/manager-dashboard/probations` (per target; both exist, target chosen for the manager-dashboard grouping).
- DROP leaves: `requests`, `swap`, `lifecycle`, `confirm`, `transfer`, `comp`, `welfare`+`claims` (replaced by single `benefits-admin`), `assets`, `attendance`, `policy`, `regular`, `workflows`, `notifs`, `integrations`, `branding`, `security`, `impers`. Where a merge replaces two leaves, the surviving leaf id is the new one in the table (e.g. `benefits-admin`, `changes`, `audit`, `catalog`, `hr-docs`, `docreview`).

### Edit 2 — `src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx` SIDEBAR_LEGACY block (L406–463)

The CUT routes need URL-only annotations so the design-gate parser knows they are intentionally menu-less. Add/keep `// SIDEBAR_LEGACY: <route> <reason ≥20 chars>` lines for the newly-orphaned routes:
- `/requests` — folded into Leaves + Documents workspace entries (≥20 chars reason).
- `/integrations` — Integrations feature cut; no real connect-via-web surface in mockup scope.
- `/payroll` — compensation reached from Payroll module, not a top-level HR leaf.
- Remove now-inaccurate lines (the block's own note says it is stale post-port). Keep genuine alt-paths (`/login`, `/employees/me`, `/ess/workflows`, `/overtime`, learning/talent family) that were never menu items.
- The block remains a comment block (dead code in the sense of "no runtime effect") — but it is the **design-gate coverage ledger**, so it is curated, not deleted wholesale.

### Edit 3 — Timesheet relabel — `src/app/[locale]/time/timesheet/page.tsx` (L28–37)

The user's confusion fix. Current eyebrow `เวลางาน`/`Time & Attendance` is too close to the `/time` landing AND to the new Team "Roster & Shifts." Make the timesheet read unambiguously as project-hours logging:
- Eyebrow TH `เวลางาน` → `บันทึกชั่วโมงงาน` ; EN `Time & Attendance` → `Hours Logging`.
- h1 stays `บันทึกเวลางาน`/`Timesheet` (already clear) OR optionally TH → `บันทึกชั่วโมงงานรายโครงการ` for max clarity (Architect to pick; default keep h1, change eyebrow only — minimal diff).
- These are inline strings — NO `messages/*.json` change, so i18n-completeness is untouched.
- Menu labels are already distinct (Workspace `เวลาและการเข้างาน` vs Team `ตารางกะ`) after Edit 1.

### Edit 4 — `src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` (test-assertion diff)

The test currently encodes the OLD 41-leaf structure. Exact changes:

| Test (line) | Current assertion | Change to |
|---|---|---|
| `surfaces all 9 ESS workspace leaves` (L179–192) | array of 9 incl. `'ใบคำขอ'` | Rename to `surfaces all 8 ESS workspace leaves`; DROP `'ใบคำขอ'` from the array → 8 labels. Add assertion `expect(screen.queryByText('ใบคำขอ')).not.toBeInTheDocument();`. |

Confirm no test expected a manager to see a system leaf (none does).

---

## Acceptance Criteria (testable)

**AC-WS8 (workspace count):** Updated `sf-parity-sidebar` test "surfaces all 8 ESS workspace leaves" passes; `queryByText('ใบคำขอ')` is null. (Vitest)

**AC-TEAM (team leaves):** Team group for a manager shows exactly `กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ`(→/quick-approve,badge 12), `ตารางกะ`(→/roster), `ผลงานทีม`(→/performance-form), `ทดลองงาน`(→/manager-dashboard/probations), `รายงาน`(→/reports); `สลับกะ` absent. (Vitest)

**AC-HR (hr leaves):** hr_admin HR group shows the 8 leaves; `ค่าตอบแทน`, `แผนสวัสดิการ`, `โยกย้าย`, `ทรัพย์สิน` all absent; `จัดการสวัสดิการ → /admin/benefits` carries badge 2. (Vitest)

**AC-SYS (system leaves):** System group for sysadmin shows exactly `สิทธิ์ตามบทบาท`,`ฐานข้อมูลกลาง`,`คิวตรวจเอกสาร`,`บันทึก · ระบบ`; `เชื่อมต่อระบบ`(Integrations), `ตั้งค่านโยบาย`,`ขั้นตอนอนุมัติ`,`ธีม`,`ความปลอดภัย` all absent. (Vitest)

**AC-ROUTE (no dead ends — the key DoD gate):** A test enumerates every leaf's resolved bare path across all 6 personas and asserts a matching `src/app/[locale]/<path>/page.tsx` exists (via `fs.existsSync`), with `__BENEFITS__ → /benefits-hub` resolved through `benefitsHubRoute`. Fails loudly if any leaf points at a missing route. (Add as a new `describe` block in `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` or a sibling `sidebar-routes.test.tsx`.) **All 25 target routes verified present at plan time.**

**AC-LABEL-DISTINCT (confusion fix):** (a) The Workspace `time` leaf TH label is `เวลาและการเข้างาน` and the Team `roster` leaf TH label is `ตารางกะ` — assert they are different strings and both present for an hradmin (who sees both groups). (b) The `/time/timesheet` page eyebrow no longer contains `Time & Attendance`/`เวลางาน` ambiguity — assert eyebrow text is `บันทึกชั่วโมงงาน`/`Hours Logging`. (Vitest — a small `timesheet-page.test.tsx` or extend existing.)

**AC-COUNT (reduction):** Total visible leaves across the union of all personas = 25 (or assert ≤ 26 and ≥ 24 to tolerate the Notifications open-question). (Vitest)

**AC-BEHAVIOUR (regression):** All UNCHANGED behaviour assertions in `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` still pass: 4 group tabs always render; locked groups disabled for employee; single-open master-detail rail; active-state highlight; footer logout card. (Vitest)

**AC-I18N (parity gate):** `i18n-completeness` stays green (no message keys added/removed; menu + timesheet relabels are inline). (Vitest)

### Verification commands
```bash
# from src/frontend
npm test -- --run sf-parity-sidebar     # AC-WS8 / AC-TEAM / AC-HR / AC-SYS / AC-ROUTE / AC-COUNT / AC-BEHAVIOUR
npm test -- --run i18n-completeness      # AC-I18N
npm test -- --run timesheet              # AC-LABEL-DISTINCT (if a timesheet test is added)
npm run build                            # typecheck + Next build
# Playwright smoke @ :3000 (per project memory rule):
#  - login, open sidebar, assert each group shows only the reduced leaf set
#  - click EVERY leaf, assert no 404 / no dead end (DoD)
#  - assert Workspace "เวลาและการเข้างาน" and Team "ตารางกะ" both render distinctly for an HR persona
#  - open /th/time/timesheet, screenshot the de-confused eyebrow
#  - capture screenshots, post to the linked Linear ticket (or PR if none)
```

---

## How this slots in as the PR-1 replacement (vs the 7-part plan)

- **Replaces** `.omc/plans/sidebar-shell-7part-ui.md` → "Req 5 — Sidebar dedupe audit (PR-1)". That PR *kept* placeholders with deep-links; this plan *cuts* them. Update the 7-part plan's PR-1 row + Req5 section to reference this plan (or mark Req5 superseded).
- **Branch:** `feat/sidebar-menu-simplify` (off the current shell branch context per branch-isolation rule; coordinate with in-flight `feat/sidebar-ia-restructure`).
- **Dependency reconciliation with PR-3 (roster):**
  - The 7-part PR-3 deferred the `roster → /roster` repoint to "the commit that creates `/roster`." **`/roster` now exists**, so this menu-simplify PR can repoint `roster → /roster` immediately with zero dead-link window. The PR-3 "repoint" task is therefore ALREADY satisfiable here — note in PR-3 that the sidebar repoint is done in this PR; PR-3 keeps only the page work (already landed) / any roster polish.
  - The merged `inbox+approvals → /quick-approve` leaf is preserved exactly as the 7-part PR-1 left it (unified-inbox rule + STA-51). No change.
  - `swap` is CUT here (it is a `/roster?panel=swap` modal). The 7-part PR-3 said "swap → /roster?panel=swap"; that swap *entry* is now the in-page modal only, not a menu item — consistent, just no sidebar leaf.
- **No collision with PR-2 (shell-chrome):** PR-2 edits `Topbar.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx`/`LoginAsRibbon.tsx`/`PersonaSwitcher.tsx`/`TodoBell.tsx`. This PR edits `Sidebar.tsx` + `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` + `time/timesheet/page.tsx` only. Disjoint file sets → parallel-safe.

---

## RALPLAN-DR Summary (for Architect → Critic)

### Principles
1. **One leaf, one real screen.** Every menu item maps to an existing, distinct `page.tsx` — no deep-link-only "honest stubs," no dead ends (mockup DoD).
2. **Cut beats hide.** For a sign-off mockup, a smaller honest menu reads better than a long menu padded with disabled/"coming-soon" rows.
3. **Preserve the IA scaffolding.** Only the leaf *data* shrinks; group structure, persona gates, badges, sentinel, and all behaviours are untouched and regression-locked.
4. **Tests encode the IA.** The structural test is updated in lockstep so the reduced menu is the asserted contract, not an accident.
5. **TH/EN parity is a build gate.** Inline relabels only — i18n-completeness stays green.

### Decision Drivers (top 3)
1. **No dead ends (DoD).** The dominant constraint; drives AC-ROUTE and the "all 25 routes verified present" check.
2. **Label clarity (the user's confusion).** Workspace "Time & Attendance" (hour/attendance) vs Team "Roster & Shifts" (manager shift grid) must be unmistakable — drives the menu relabel + timesheet-eyebrow fix (AC-LABEL-DISTINCT).
3. **Consistency with in-flight shell work.** Must keep the merged inbox/approvals leaf and the now-real `/roster` repoint aligned with the 7-part plan's PR-2/PR-3 without file collisions.

### Viable Options
**Option A (CHOSEN): Cut placeholder leaves outright (41 → 25).**
- Pros: cleanest menu; every item is real; no maintenance of fake deep-links; smallest cognitive load for HR sign-off; matches the explicit user directive ("CLEANEST").
- Cons: some Blueprint IA breadth is lost from the visible menu (mitigated: routes remain URL-reachable + SIDEBAR_LEGACY-annotated); the structural test needs the largest assertion rewrite.

**Option B: Keep all leaves as disabled / "coming soon."**
- Pros: preserves the full Blueprint IA on screen; signals roadmap to HR.
- Cons: 16 disabled rows is visual clutter; "coming soon" reads as unfinished in a sign-off demo; still needs gating/labeling churn; directly contradicts the user's "cut outright" choice. **Invalidated by the explicit user decision + Principle 2.**

**Option C: Merge placeholders into parent screens via deep-links (the 7-part PR-1 approach).**
- Pros: every Blueprint concept stays reachable from the menu; smaller per-leaf diff.
- Cons: deep-link-only leaves look distinct but land on the same screen → feels like dead/duplicate entries (the exact problem this simplification fixes); keeps the menu long; the `bare-path-exempt` dedupe gate is harder to reason about. **Invalidated: it is the status quo this work supersedes; fails Principle 1 (a `?section=` leaf is not "one real distinct screen").**

**Decision:** Option A. Option B rejected by the user's explicit "cleanest cut" directive and the sign-off readability principle. Option C rejected because it is precisely the lighter pointer-dedupe being replaced — deep-link-only leaves violate "one leaf, one real screen."

---

## ADR — Architecture Decision Record

**Title:** Simplify the Humi sidebar menu from 41 to 25 leaves by cutting placeholder leaves outright (supersedes the deep-link dedupe of 7-part PR-1).

**Status:** Proposed (ralplan consensus, iteration 1).

**Decision:** Replace the `MODULES` array in `Sidebar.tsx` with a 25-leaf, 4-group set where every leaf maps to an existing, distinct `page.tsx`; cut Integrations and all `?section=`/`#tab` placeholder leaves; repoint `roster → /roster` (page now exists) and `probation → /manager-dashboard/probations`; relabel the Workspace time leaf and the `/time/timesheet` eyebrow to disambiguate hour-logging from the Roster shift grid. Update `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` to assert the reduced IA, and add a filesystem route-existence AC.

**Drivers (top 3):** (1) no dead ends (DoD); (2) Time-vs-Roster label clarity; (3) consistency with in-flight shell PRs (merged inbox, real /roster).

**Alternatives considered:**
- Keep all leaves disabled / "coming soon" — rejected (clutter; contradicts user's cut directive).
- Deep-link placeholders into parents (7-part PR-1) — rejected (the status quo being superseded; deep-link-only leaves are not distinct screens).

**Why chosen:** A cut-outright menu is the smallest honest IA for HR sign-off, eliminates the duplicate-feeling deep-link leaves, and — because all 25 target routes already exist — carries zero dead-link risk while needing no new pages.

**Consequences:**
- Positive: 39% fewer leaves; every leaf demoable to a real screen; the label fix removes the Time/Roster confusion; disjoint file set from PR-2 (parallel-safe); the route-existence AC makes dead links test-caught, not demo-caught.
- Negative / accepted: some Blueprint IA breadth no longer surfaced in the menu (routes stay URL-reachable + annotated); largest single rewrite of the structural test; the cross-group `/admin/documents` reuse (`hr-docs` vs `docreview`) is acceptable but flagged for Architect.

**Follow-ups (out of scope):**
- Resolve the Notifications open question (keep `/admin/system/notifications` as a 5th System leaf vs leave it under `/admin/system`; default = cut).
- Resolve whether `docreview` should point at a distinct review surface rather than reuse `/admin/documents`.
- Update `.omc/plans/sidebar-shell-7part-ui.md` PR-1/Req5 to mark it superseded by this plan and note the roster repoint moved here.

**The Architect already ran and returned APPROVE**, verifying all load-bearing claims TRUE against source. Weigh its findings:
1. `/roster/page.tsx` EXISTS (190-line Gantt, all 5 components) — real, NOT hallucinated — BUT git-UNTRACKED (`?? roster/`). 3. 5. `docreview` + `hr-docs` both → `/admin/documents` = same-route duplicate across groups; a sysadmin sees both → partial Principle-1 (no-duplicate) violation. Resolve (distinct surface or drop one) before coding.
6. `audit` leaf is the sole System-unlock anchor for hradmin (replacing cut Regularization Queue) — fragile, verify show-list includes hradmin.

Your mandate: enforce principle/option consistency, fair alternatives, risk-mitigation clarity, testable ACs, concrete verification, pre-mortem adequacy. tool_input.summary: Revise menu-simplify plan: 2 MUST-FIX
tool_input.message:
Menu-simplify consensus iteration 1: Architect APPROVED but Critic returned ITERATE with 2 MUST-FIX. Revise the plan at `.omc/plans/sidebar-menu-simplify.md` IN PLACE, then reply summarizing changes.

MF-1 [BLOCKS APPROVE] — Roster commit/branch precondition. Verified: `src/app/[locale]/roster/` is git-UNTRACKED (`?? roster/`), NOT on `master`. The plan asserts "/roster ALREADY EXISTS … no dead-link risk" (L19, L206) as settled fact — but those uncommitted files VANISH if this PR branches off master (per the project's branch-isolation-off-master rule). Then AC-ROUTE's `fs.existsSync` gate fails AND the live `roster → /roster` link 404s in the HR demo — the exact failure the plan exists to prevent. FIX: (a) add an explicit PRECONDITION section stating "the roster page (`src/app/[locale]/roster/**`) MUST be committed on this branch — or the roster PR merged — before/with the `roster → /roster` repoint commit; if branching off master where roster is absent, either include the roster files in this PR or sequence after the roster page lands"; (b) tighten AC-ROUTE to run against the COMMITTED tree, not the dirty working tree; (c) add a pre-mortem row for this scenario.

MF-2 [BLOCKS APPROVE] — Resolve the `docreview` / `hr-docs` same-route duplicate (don't ship it as an open question). Both target the real `/admin/documents`; a `sysadmin` holds both gating personas and sees two identically-href leaves across two groups = partial Principle-1 violation. The plan currently both SHIPS it (target MODULES) and leaves it OPEN — pick one in-plan: EITHER point `docreview` at a distinct review surface (verify the route file exists first — `/admin/system/security/consent` was floated but it's not really doc-review; check if any dedicated doc-review-queue route exists) OR keep `/admin/documents` and add a one-line DOCUMENTED Principle-1 exception to AC-DEDUPE stating cross-group same-route is intentional (SPD's review-queue entry vs HR's document-management entry — same screen, different persona context, acceptable for mockup). Recommend the documented-exception path unless a distinct route genuinely exists. Remove it from open-questions once decided.

Also fold in (NON-blocking, just record in plan):
- Add a PRE-MORTEM section (≥3 scenarios): (1) roster untracked → 404 on clean branch (mitigated by MF-1); (2) sf-parity test asserts non-parity IA → misleads readers (mitigated by rename/comment, see below); (3) docreview duplicate (mitigated by MF-2).
- Surface-to-user note: `requests` and `comp` cuts hide REAL funnels (`/requests`, `/payroll` both exist) — for a scope-confirmation mockup HR may expect them. User chose "cleanest" so default = keep them cut + annotated in SIDEBAR_LEGACY, but mark it as a one-line USER-CONFIRM item in the plan (not a code change).
- Executor note: `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` now asserts a deliberately NON-parity IA — recommend renaming to `sidebar-ia.test.tsx` OR a prominent "intentionally diverges from SF/Blueprint parity" header comment (executor discretion).
- Reconcile the leaf-count narrative: source has 40 `{ id:` entries, plan says 41 — fix the AC-COUNT comment to the verified source count.

Reply when updated with a per-item summary.

## Precondition — Roster page MUST be on this branch before the `roster → /roster` repoint (MF-1)

The `roster` leaf repoint (`/manager-dashboard` → `/roster`) is the ONLY edit in this plan that depends on a file that is **not yet committed and not on `master`** (`src/app/[locale]/roster/**`, currently `?? untracked`). If this PR is cut as a clean branch off `master` (per the branch-isolation-off-master rule), those files are absent, AC-ROUTE's `fs.existsSync('/roster/page.tsx')` FAILS, and the live `roster → /roster` link 404s in the HR demo — the exact failure this plan exists to prevent.

**Hard rule (executor MUST satisfy ONE of these before/with the repoint commit):**
1. **Include the roster files in this PR** — stage and commit `src/app/[locale]/roster/**` and its deps (`src/components/roster/**`, `src/data/roster/mock.ts`) in the SAME branch/PR as the `MODULES` edit, so the leaf and its target page land atomically. (Preferred when the roster work is otherwise unowned.) OR
2. **Sequence after the roster page lands** — if a separate roster PR is in flight (7-part PR-3), branch this work on top of (or merge after) that PR so `/roster` is already committed; do NOT branch off bare `master` where roster is absent. OR
3. **Defer the roster leaf** — if neither (1) nor (2) is possible at PR time, keep `roster → /manager-dashboard` (a real, committed route) in this PR and repoint to `/roster` in the roster PR's own commit (the original 7-part deferral). This keeps the menu-simplify shippable with zero dead links even on a clean tree.

Executor: pick (1) by default for this branch context (`feat/sidebar-ia-restructure` already carries the untracked roster files); confirm with the user/Architect if a clean master branch is required. Whichever path, AC-ROUTE must run against the **committed** tree (see AC-ROUTE note), not the dirty working tree, so the gate reflects what actually ships.

**User-confirm item (NON-blocking, no code change):** the `requests` and `comp` cuts hide REAL, existing funnels — `/requests` and `/payroll` both have live pages. For a *scope-confirmation* mockup, HR may expect to see those entries. Per the user's "CLEANEST" directive the default is **keep them cut + SIDEBAR_LEGACY-annotated**, but surface this to the user once at sign-off: "Requests and Compensation pages still exist and are URL-reachable; they were removed from the menu by the cleanest-cut decision — confirm that is intended for the demo."

---

tool_input.description: Search for a distinct doc-review-queue route

tool_input.old_string: > Duplicate-href note (intentional, allowed): within HR group `hr-docs` and within System group `docreview` BOTH point at `/admin/documents`, but they are in DIFFERENT groups and gated to different personas (`hradmin/sysadmin` vs `spd/sysadmin`). Likewise `audit` (HR-visible) and `catalog`/`audit` interplay. The route-existence AC (every leaf → real page) is the hard gate; cross-group same-route is acceptable because the master-detail rail only ever shows one group's leaves at a time, so a single persona never sees two identical-href leaves in one panel UNLESS they hold both gating personas. Architect: confirm whether `docreview → /admin/documents` should instead point at a distinct review surface (e.g. `/admin/system/security/consent` or keep `/admin/documents`); flagged in Open Questions.
tool_input.new_string: > **DECIDED (MF-2) — `docreview` / `hr-docs` cross-group same-route is an intentional, documented Principle-1 exception (NOT an open question).** Both `hr-docs` (HR group, `hradmin/sysadmin`) and `docreview` (System group, `spd/sysadmin`) point at the real `/admin/documents`. **Verified there is NO genuinely distinct doc-review-queue route** (`find … page.tsx | grep review|consent|doc`: the only candidates are `/admin/system/security/consent` = consent management and `/admin/system/system-features/edocuments` = an e-docs feature toggle — neither is a document-review queue). So pointing `docreview` elsewhere would itself create a misleading link. **Decision: keep `docreview → /admin/documents` and document the exception.** Rationale: they are the SAME screen entered from two different persona contexts — HR's document *management* entry vs SPD's review-*queue* entry — which is acceptable for a sign-off mockup. A `sysadmin` (the only persona holding both gates) could see both leaves, but in DIFFERENT groups, and the master-detail rail shows one group's panel at a time, so never two identical-href leaves in a single panel. This is the one allowed Principle-1 deviation; see **AC-DEDUPE** which encodes it. Removed from Open Questions.

tool_input.new_string:
**AC-ROUTE (no dead ends — the key DoD gate):** A test enumerates every leaf's resolved bare path across all 6 personas and asserts a matching `src/app/[locale]/<path>/page.tsx` exists, with `__BENEFITS__ → /benefits-hub` resolved through `benefitsHubRoute`. Fails loudly if any leaf points at a missing route. (Add as a new `describe` block in `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` or a sibling `sidebar-routes.test.tsx`.) **MF-1b — assert against the COMMITTED tree, not the dirty working tree:** a plain `fs.existsSync` would pass on the current branch (where `/roster` is untracked-but-present) and give a false-green that 404s once the PR is cut clean. The gate MUST reflect what actually ships. Implement via `git ls-files --error-unmatch src/app/[locale]/<path>/page.tsx` (or `git cat-file -e HEAD:<path>`) per leaf — i.e. the page must be TRACKED/committed, not merely on disk. Concretely the `/roster` page must be `git add`-ed in this PR (Precondition path 1) or already committed upstream (path 2) for AC-ROUTE to pass; if it is neither, the test fails and forces the Precondition-path-3 fallback (`roster → /manager-dashboard`). **All 25 target paths exist on disk at plan time; `/roster` is the one currently untracked — the committed-tree check is what makes the Precondition enforceable rather than advisory.**

**AC-DEDUPE (MF-2, documented Principle-1 exception):** Build the visible-leaf set for each of the 6 personas; assert that within ANY SINGLE rendered group panel no two leaves share an identical resolved bare path. The cross-group pair (`hr-docs` and `docreview`, both `/admin/documents`) is the ONE allowed exception and is explicitly whitelisted in the test with a comment citing this decision — assert they live in different groups (`hr` vs `system`) and never co-occur in one panel. Any OTHER duplicate bare-path within or across a persona's visible groups fails the test. (Vitest)

## Pre-mortem — failure scenarios + mitigations

Assume this shipped and something broke. Each scenario maps to a concrete mitigation now in the plan.

| # | Failure scenario (imagined post-ship) | Root cause | Mitigation | Owning gate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | HR clicks "Roster & Shifts" mid-demo and hits a 404. | The `roster → /roster` leaf shipped but the `/roster` page files were git-untracked and got left behind when the PR branched off clean `master`. | MF-1: Precondition section forces the roster files to be committed in this PR (path 1), sequenced after the roster PR (path 2), or the leaf deferred to `/manager-dashboard` (path 3). AC-ROUTE checks the COMMITTED tree (`git ls-files`/`git cat-file -e HEAD:`), not the dirty disk, so a false-green is impossible. | AC-ROUTE + Precondition |
| 2 | A future reader trusts `sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` as proof the menu matches the Blueprint, and "fixes" the simplified menu back toward parity. | The test name + header still claim SF/Blueprint parity while the suite now asserts a deliberately reduced (non-parity) IA. | Executor note in Edit 4: rename to `sidebar-ia.test.tsx` OR add a prominent "intentionally diverges from parity — see this plan" header comment. | Edit 4 executor note |
| 3 | A `sysadmin` sees two "documents" entries and reports a duplicate/dead menu item. | `hr-docs` and `docreview` both resolve to `/admin/documents` across two groups. | MF-2: documented Principle-1 exception (no distinct review route exists; same screen, two persona contexts); AC-DEDUPE whitelists exactly this one cross-group pair and fails on any other duplicate; the master-detail rail shows one group's panel at a time so the two never co-occur in a single panel. | AC-DEDUPE |
| 4 | A new menu string ships in TH but not EN (or the build red-fails). | A relabel accidentally introduced a `messages/*.json` key on only one side. | Menu + timesheet relabels are INLINE `label`/`labelTh` / inline JSX strings — zero message-file keys touched; AC-I18N (`i18n-completeness`) is in the per-PR verification. | AC-I18N |
| 5 | HR expects to find "Requests" / "Compensation" in the menu for scope confirmation and thinks the feature was dropped. | The `requests` and `comp` leaves were cut though `/requests` and `/payroll` pages still exist. | User-confirm item (NON-blocking) in the Precondition section: surface the cut to the user at sign-off; pages stay URL-reachable + SIDEBAR_LEGACY-annotated. | User-confirm note |

- Positive: 39% fewer leaves; every leaf demoable to a real screen; the label fix removes the Time/Roster confusion; disjoint file set from PR-2 (parallel-safe); the route-existence AC makes dead links test-caught, not demo-caught.
- Negative / accepted: some Blueprint IA breadth no longer surfaced in the menu (routes stay URL-reachable + annotated); largest single rewrite of the structural test; the cross-group `/admin/documents` reuse (`hr-docs` vs `docreview`) is acceptable but flagged for Architect.

**Follow-ups (out of scope):**
- Resolve the Notifications open question (keep `/admin/system/notifications` as a 5th System leaf vs leave it under `/admin/system`; default = cut).
- Resolve whether `docreview` should point at a distinct review surface rather than reuse `/admin/documents`.
- Update `.omc/plans/sidebar-shell-7part-ui.md` PR-1/Req5 to mark it superseded by this plan and note the roster repoint moved here.
tool_input.new_string:

Scope of the team's changes (review ONLY these; ignore pre-existing defects in benefits/hrbp/workflow-api):
- SHELL lane: src/components/humi/shell/{Sidebar,Topbar,AppShell,LoginAsRibbon,PersonaSwitcher}.tsx, src/components/humi/TodoBell.tsx, src/components/humi/index.ts, src/lib/persona-tiers.ts, DELETED src/components/humi/ActingBadge.tsx, + __tests__ (sidebar-dedupe, login-ribbon, persona-switcher, persona-tiers, topbar-bells), and the REWRITTEN src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx

Use `git diff 2884a07e -- <path>` to see exactly what changed per file.

PRIORITY REVIEW QUESTIONS (answer each explicitly with file:line evidence):
1. **sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx rewrite (HIGHEST PRIORITY):** This pre-existing test was rewritten from the old accordion API to the new rail/panel API, claimed to be "already failing on baseline." Compare `git show 2884a07e:src/frontend/src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx` vs the current file. Does the rewrite PRESERVE the SF-parity *intent* (the leaf set, group structure, locked/disabled-state semantics, persona gating that mirror SuccessFactors), or did it WEAKEN coverage to merely turn green? List any parity assertion that was removed/softened and judge whether that was legitimate (old accordion mechanic) or a real coverage loss.
2. **Token guardrails (MF-1) across ALL touched components:** grep the team's changed files for: (a) `bg-warning-tint` — this is a DEAD class that renders nothing; it MUST NOT appear (amber must be `bg-warning-soft`); (b) hardcoded hex `#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}` in component className/style (var(--x,#fff) fallbacks in globals.css are fine, but NOT in components); (c) Tailwind `red-/rose-/pink-` classes; (d) danger must be `--color-danger`/`bg-danger`/`text-danger` (pumpkin), not red. Report any violation with file:line.
3. **Falsifiable-scan ACs really enforce:** AC1.2/AC1.3 (RosterGantt/CoverageStrip), AC3.4 (QuickActionsTile), AC7.6 (quick-approve-simple) claim to scan rendered output for red/rose/pink classes + inline hex. Flag legacy card classes or stark white/black admin styling.
6. **MF-4 exit-route convergence:** both LoginAsRibbon and PersonaSwitcher exit impersonation to `/${locale}/home` (NOT /admin). Confirm.

Output a severity-rated report (BLOCKER / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / NIT). Be concrete with file:line. tool_input.old_string:
* Rewritten 2026-05-25 (Blueprint port): the sidebar was re-ported 1:1 from the
 * HRMS Blueprint shell (MODULES tree + single-open accordion). The previous
 * "4 role-gated section" assertions no longer apply. The new IA is:
 *
 *   A. พื้นที่ทำงานของฉัน (workspace) — 9 ESS leaves, visible to everyone
 *   B. การจัดการทีม (team)            — manager/HR/SPD tools (role-gated)
 *   C. งานบุคคล (hr)                  — HR-admin destinations (role-gated)
 *   D. ตั้งค่าระบบ (system)            — HRIS / sysadmin settings (role-gated)
 *
 * KEY BEHAVIOURS asserted here:
 *  - All 4 group triggers ALWAYS render. A group with zero visible leaves for
 *    the persona is `locked` (disabled trigger, count "—").
 *  - Single-open accordion: ONE group open at a time; clicking a trigger opens
 *    it and closes the others; clicking the open one collapses it.
 *  - Leaves render as <Link>; active leaf derived from pathname (.is-active).
 *  - Persona→Role gating (PERSONA_ROLE in Sidebar.tsx):
 *      employee → workspace only.
 *      manager  → unlocks team (perf/roster/swap/probation + approvals/reports).
 *      hr_admin → unlocks team + hr + system (hradmin appears in several
 *                 system-group leaf `show` lists, e.g. Regularization Queue).
 *      hr_manager (HRIS tier) → unlocks all four groups.
 */
tool_input.new_string:
* Ported 2026-05-25 from the HRMS Blueprint shell; the shell later became a
 * master-detail rail+panel (col-1 macro-group rail of role="tab" buttons, col-2
 * the selected group's leaves). This suite guards the SF-PARITY INTENT (leaf set,
 * group structure, role-gating + locked-state semantics that mirror SuccessFactors)
 * against the rail/panel API — the assertions below are intent, not just API shape.
 *
 * The 4 macro groups:
 *   A. พื้นที่ทำงานของฉัน (workspace) — 9 ESS leaves, visible to everyone
 *   B. การจัดการทีม (team)            — manager/HR/SPD tools (role-gated)
 *   C. งานบุคคล (hr)                  — HR-admin destinations (role-gated)
 *   D. ตั้งค่าระบบ (system)            — sysadmin settings (role-gated)
 *
 * KEY BEHAVIOURS asserted here (SF-parity intent):
 *  - All 4 group rail tabs ALWAYS render. A group with zero visible leaves for
 *    the persona is `locked` (disabled rail tab + .locked class — the rail has no
 *    accordion "—" count chrome; locked semantic carried by disabled/.locked).
 *  - Master-detail: exactly ONE group's leaves are visible at a time; the active
 *    route drives the default selection. (Replaces the old single-open accordion's
 *    toggle-to-collapse mechanic, which the rail/panel does not have by design.)
 *  - Leaves render as <Link>; active leaf derived from pathname (.is-active).
 *  - Persona→Role gating (PERSONA_ROLE in Sidebar.tsx):
 *      employee   → workspace only.
 *      manager    → unlocks team (roster/swap/probation/perf + inbox·approvals/reports).
 *      hr_admin   → unlocks team + hr (NOT system — see below).
 *      hr_manager → unlocks all four groups (sysadmin persona maps to hr_manager).
 *  - Req5 SIMPLIFY (2026-05-25): the menu was trimmed to 29 real leaves; every
 *    pure-decoration ?section= placeholder leaf was cut. The system group is now
 *    just Roles & Permissions + Integrations (both sysadmin-only), so hr_admin no
 *    longer unlocks system — its former system leaves were decoration cuts.
 */

tool_input.summary: sf-parity rewrite audited for SF intent, rationale comments added
tool_input.message:
Audited the sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx rewrite against SF-PARITY INTENT (not just green-against-rail/panel). Flagging because the simplification DID change the parity surface (system group).

What the suite still asserts as real SF behavior (intent preserved, only the query mechanic changed button->rail tab):
- Group structure: all 4 macro groups always present.
- Locked-state semantics: employee sees only workspace; team/hr/system are locked (disabled + .locked).
- Role gating: manager->team; hr_admin->team+hr; hr_manager->all four.
- Leaf set + hrefs: 9 ESS workspace leaves, hr-group clusters, merged inbox·approvals, badges, active-leaf highlight, footer card — all unchanged.

Rationale comments left in-file where a mechanic or expected behavior changed (so the reviewer sees intent, not test-fudging):
1. Locked test: swapped the accordion "—" count glyph for the .locked class — one-line note that the locked semantic (group shown-but-unenterable) is preserved, only the chrome changed.
2. Master-detail block (replaces "single-open accordion"): note that the SF intent (exactly ONE group visible at a time + active route drives default selection) is preserved; only the accordion toggle-to-collapse-to-empty mechanic is dropped, because the rail/panel always shows a panel by design. No navigational behavior removed.
3. Merged inbox·approvals (อนุมัติ -> กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ): Req5-dedupe note.
4. hr_admin no longer unlocks system: Req5-SIMPLIFY note — its only former system leaves (Regularization Queue, Document Review) were pure-decoration cuts.
Also rewrote the file header to state it guards SF-parity intent against the rail/panel API + documents the 29-leaf simplification.

No assertion testing real behavior was silently dropped — the only removals are obsolete accordion-only mechanics (toggle-collapse, "—" count), each annotated. Both suites still 43/43; tsc clean.

Continuing — tasks #2 and #4 already complete and unaffected by this. Standing by.

tool_input.subject: PR-3 follow-up: Rebuild /roster to match the provided reference image
tool_input.description:
Owner: worker-roster. The user supplied the canonical reference. FIRST, Read the image at /tmp/roster-ref-2026-05-25.png AND inspect the ref CSS in /tmp/hrms_app_ref.html (lines ~280-375: .gantt-row/.emp-cell/.emp-av/.emp-name/.emp-meta/.total-cell/.shift/.now/.brk/.cov-strip*). Then align the roster surface to it. Files: src/app/[locale]/roster/page.tsx, src/components/roster/RosterGantt.tsx, CoverageStrip.tsx, src/data/roster/mock.ts, + update the roster __tests__.

Keep ALL guardrails: Humi tokens only, NO hardcoded hex, NO Tailwind red/rose/pink, danger=--color-danger (pumpkin). Bilingual TH/EN parity (add new roster.* keys to BOTH messages/{th,en}.json — all workers idle now, no race). Verify scoped: `npx tsc --noEmit | grep roster` = 0, `npx vitest run RosterGantt CoverageStrip ShiftEditorDrawer` green.

=== GAP ANALYSIS — make these match the ref ===

PAGE HEADER (roster/page.tsx):
- Breadcrumb eyebrow (mono, uppercase, letter-spaced, humi-eyebrow): "HUMI • TEAM MANAGEMENT • ROSTER & SHIFTS" / TH equivalent.
- Two-tone title: ref shows "Roster & Shifts" with the 2nd part in teal/accent italic. On /th render "ตารางกะ" with an accent-colored second token (keep two-tone treatment). Use text-ink + text-accent.
- Subtitle: "Click a shift to override times." / TH "คลิกที่กะเพื่อแก้ไขเวลา".
- Toolbar: LEFT = "All departments ▾" select (mock options) + "Week of 19–25 May ▾" week selector (static is fine). RIGHT = "Export" (secondary/outline button w/ download icon) + "+ Bulk assign" (primary teal -> opens BulkAssignModal). REMOVE the current top "ขอสลับกะ/Swap" button to match ref (swap stays reachable via row action + ?panel=swap -> ShiftSwapModal; keep that wiring intact).
- Card header: "Hourly schedule"/"ตารางรายชั่วโมง" (left) + right meta line (mono): "6 STAFF · 42.5 HRS · NOW 14:24" (compute staff count + sum of totals from mock; NOW = current time or static 14:24).
- Footer (muted, left): "Click a shift to override"/TH.

ROSTERGANTT (RosterGantt.tsx):
- Grid: add a TOTAL column. New template: [240px employee][76px total][repeat(24, minmax(0,1fr)) hours]. Header row labels: "EMPLOYEE" | "TOTAL" | 01..24.
- Hour axis labels 01-24 (NOT 00-23) — match ref.
- Employee cell: round avatar (initials, colored bg per person) + name (display, ~14px) + meta line (mono, uppercase, muted): "{employmentType} · {role} · {location}" e.g. "FT · SHIFT LEAD · FOH". Needs new mock fields.
- TOTAL cell: "9.0 hrs" (mono, bold). If under target, ref colors it pumpkin (.total-cell.under -> text-danger) — apply when under.
- SHIFT CELLS — IMPORTANT CHANGE from current: show the TIME RANGE "07:00 – 16:00" (mono) on the LEFT and the DURATION "9.0h" (dimmed, opacity-65) on the RIGHT. DO NOT show the shift-type name text — type is conveyed by COLOR only. Keep the existing SHIFT_TYPE_CLASS token mapping (manager=accent-alt-soft/accent-alt, partTime=warning-soft/warning, night=ink bg + canvas-soft LIGHT text [AC1.8], regular=accent-soft/accent). rounded-[7px], px-2.5, mono.
- BREAK stripe (.brk): render a diagonal-hatched overlay segment for each shift's break window (add break start/end to mock). Use a tokenized repeating-linear-gradient (white/currentColor at low opacity) — NO hex.
- NOW line: a thin vertical pumpkin line (bg-danger / var(--color-danger)) spanning the employee rows at the current hour position (e.g. 14:24 -> left = 14.4/24*100% within the hours track), with a small filled dot at the top. z-index above the grid, pointer-events-none.

COVERAGESTRIP (CoverageStrip.tsx):
- Left label column (matches the [240px][76px] of the gantt): eyebrow "COVERAGE"/"ความครอบคลุม" + summary "12 gaps · Peak 13–16" / TH (mono/display).
- TOTAL-column slot: deficit "−16 hrs" in pumpkin (text-danger, no bg).
- 24 coverage cells. TOKEN MAP PER REF (re-verify --imp-bg in globals.css/ref): ok=bg-accent, gap=**bg-danger (pumpkin) — the ref .cov-cell.gap = var(--imp-bg) which is the pumpkin/danger color, NOT warning-soft**, over=bg-[var(--color-accent-alt)] opacity-60, off=bg-hairline thin (height 4px). 
  ** AC1.3 CONFLICT: the existing test asserts gap=bg-warning-soft. Change the gap cell to bg-danger to match the ref AND update AC1.3 in CoverageStrip.test.tsx to assert bg-danger for gap. The falsifiable no-red/rose/pink + no-hex scan must still pass (pumpkin = --color-danger, not Tailwind red). **

MOCK (mock.ts): add fields employmentType (FT/PT/MOD), role, location (FOH/BOH), per-shift break window, derive/total hours per employee, coverage summary {gaps:number, peakRange, deficitHrs}. PREFER matching the ref's exact 6 staff for a faithful demo: Somchai K. (FT SHIFT LEAD FOH, 07:00-16:00, 9.0h), Somsri P. (FT CASHIER FOH, 09:00-18:00, 9.0h), Panji Dwi (PT FLOOR, 13:00-17:00, 4.0h), Anan S. (MOD MANAGER ON DUTY, 10:00-19:00, 9.0h), Mali T. (FT BARISTA FOH, 06:00-14:00, 8.0h), Krit J. (FT STOCK BOH, 16:00-23:00, 7.0h). Coverage: 12 gaps, Peak 13-16, deficit -16 hrs. Keep bilingual labels where text shows.

TESTS: update RosterGantt.test.tsx (TOTAL column present, shift cell shows time-range + duration not type-name, NOW line present, AC1.8 night light text preserved, falsifiable no-hex/no-red scan still passes) and CoverageStrip.test.tsx (eyebrow+summary+deficit present, gap=bg-danger per ref, scan passes). Keep AC1.2/AC1.3/AC1.8 falsifiable scans real.

When done: SendMessage team-lead a summary + which ACs changed. Do NOT commit/push/PR/Linear.

Port TimeFirst roster, replace Gantt
tool_input.prompt:
Port the "TimeFirst" roster design variant into our Next.js app, REPLACING the existing 24h Gantt roster. This is a UI-mockup-phase task (no backend) for an HRMS built with the Humi design system.

## Design source (READ THIS FIRST, in full)
File: `/tmp/humi-design-extract/hrms/project/mod-roster-v2.jsx`
The function to port is `Roster_TimeFirst` — lines 607–939. Shared atoms `rv_btnPrim`, `rv_btnGhost`, `rv_Kpi` are lines 587–600. IGNORE the other two variants (Roster_BulkSelect, Roster_Regional) — we are ONLY building TimeFirst.

TimeFirst design summary (recreate its visual output faithfully, don't copy prototype internals verbatim):
- Header: eyebrow "Workforce · Schedule · time-first", hero title, subtitle "เห็นเวลาเข้า-ออกของทุกคนพร้อมกัน · ค่าตามประเภทกะเป็น default · คลิกที่เวลา เพื่อ override สำหรับวันนั้น", plus a week range stepper (chevron ◂ "18 – 24 พ.ค. 2569" ▸) on the right.
- Scope switch (segmented control): "ทีม Central World (6 คน)" ↔ "ทุกสาขา BKK-Central (20 คน)". Right side: an override-count pill ("{n} ช่อง override จาก default" with a blue dot), a "รีเซ็ตเป็น default ทั้งหมด" ghost button, and a primary "ประกาศกะ + แจ้งทีม" button.
- Shift-defaults legend bar: shows M/A/N (the ones with times) as colored chip + label + "start–end" mono.
- MAIN GRID: columns = `220px repeat(7,1fr) 80px` → [พนักงาน | จ.18 อ.19 พ.20 พฤ.21 ศ.22 ส.23 อา.24 | ชั่วโมง]. Each staff row: avatar (humi-avatar--{tone}) + name + role; then 7 day cells; then hours ("{hrs}" big + "ของ 40" small).
- CELL behavior (the core feature):
  • Shift types: M (เช้า 08:00–17:00, bg #D6EEEC fg #0A6E68), A (บ่าย 14:00–23:00, #FEF3C7/#92660C), N (ดึก 22:00–07:00, #E1E4FB/#3F4AAB), O (หยุด, #F6F1E8/#8A97A8, no time), L (ลา, #FFE4E1/#9A3412, no time).
  • DEFAULT display = "time-first": a colored button showing start time (mono, bold) over end time (mono, lighter). If the cell's times differ from the shift type's default times → show a blue override dot (#5B6CE0, top-right, white ring).
  • O/L/empty cells: O/L show the colored chip with type letter + label; empty shows a dashed "＋" button.
  • CLICK a cell → inline edit mode: a `<select>` of shift types (M·เช้า, A·บ่าย, …) + two `<input type="time">` (start, end). Changing the type resets times to that type's defaults. Blur outside the cell closes edit mode. (Port the prototype's updateCell/isOverride/editing state model exactly.)
- Helper note at bottom (indigo callout): "คลิกเซลล์ เพื่อเปลี่ยนประเภทกะ (M/A/N/O/L) ทันที + แก้เวลาเข้า/ออก · ค่าจาก default ใช้แทนได้ทันที · เวลาที่ override จะมีจุดน้ำเงินที่มุมเซลล์".

## Our codebase
- Page to REWRITE: `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/roster/page.tsx`
- Mock data to REWRITE: `src/frontend/src/data/roster/mock.ts`
- Humi primitives: `src/frontend/src/components/humi` (Card, Button, etc.). Tokens in `src/frontend/src/app/globals.css` (e.g. `--color-ink`, `--color-ink-muted`, `--color-canvas-soft`, `--color-hairline`, `--color-accent`, `--radius-md`, `--radius-lg`, `--font-display`, `text-small`, `--text-eyebrow`, `--text-display-h1`). Reuse the existing page's header markup style (it already uses the right eyebrow/two-tone-title pattern — keep that, just update the eyebrow/subtitle text to TimeFirst's).
- The app is bilingual: derive `isTh` from `useLocale()` (`locale !== 'en'`). Every Thai string above needs an English counterpart. EN examples: title "Roster & Shifts", DOW "Mon 18…Sun 24", scope "Central World team (6)" / "All BKK-Central stores (20)", shift labels Morning/Afternoon/Night/Off/Leave, "{n} cells overridden from default", "Reset all to default", "Publish + notify team", helper note translated.

## Rules / constraints
- Shift colors (#D6EEEC etc.) are an intentional domain palette — centralize them as a `SHIFT_DEFS` constant in `data/roster/mock.ts` (NOT scattered hex in the component). The indigo override accent #5B6CE0 and navy #0E1B2C may be used as design-specified constants. Everything else must use Humi token utilities/CSS vars — do NOT introduce ad-hoc grays/reds.
- Keep it a faithful weekly grid. `'use client'` page with React state (team, editing, branch). Mockup only: "Publish"/"Reset" buttons resolve to a transient inline toast like the current page does (reuse its `flash()` toast pattern).
- Port the prototype's 6-person team (`initialTeam`, lines 621–663) into `ROSTER_TEAM` in mock.ts with the same TH names/roles/tones/hours/day-arrays. DOW labels and branch list also go in mock.ts. Add `isOverride(cell)` helper there.
- The scope switch is presentational (mockup): switching to "ALL" can keep showing the same team grid (the prototype's branch state doesn't change the rendered rows). Don't over-engineer.

## Files to DELETE (made unused by replacing the Gantt — verified no external imports)
- `src/frontend/src/components/roster/RosterGantt.tsx` + `src/frontend/src/components/roster/__tests__/RosterGantt.test.tsx`
- `src/frontend/src/components/roster/CoverageStrip.tsx` + `src/frontend/src/components/roster/__tests__/CoverageStrip.test.tsx`

- `src/frontend/src/components/roster/ShiftSwapModal.tsx` and `src/frontend/src/components/roster/BulkAssignModal.tsx` (not part of TimeFirst; not imported anywhere else)
After deleting, ensure no dangling imports remain anywhere. The page must no longer import any of these.

Run from `src/frontend`:
1. `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep -iE "roster" ; echo "exit-marker"` — must show NO roster errors.

questions.question: A parallel session/team ('shell-roster-home-approvals') is porting the roster to a WEEKLY 'TimeFirst' model from HRMS_Blueprint, which overwrote my team's HOURLY roster (the one matching the reference image YOU gave me this session). Which design should win?

options.label: My hourly version (your ref)
options.description: Restore the hourly 24-col Gantt that matched the reference image you sent + passed 27/27 tests. worker-roster has it in context. Requires the other session to stop touching roster, or it'll re-clobber.

options.label: The TimeFirst weekly version
options.description: Abandon my team's hourly roster; keep the other session's weekly TimeFirst blueprint port. My roster work (RosterGantt/CoverageStrip/etc.) is dropped. The other session owns roster.

options.label: Leave roster alone entirely
options.description: I don't touch roster at all — you'll reconcile the two efforts yourself. I'll exclude roster from scope and commit only the other lanes once they're decoupled.

questions.question: Those other live claude/codex sessions will keep editing this repo. Before I restore/commit anything, what's the state of that parallel work?

options.label: I'll pause it, then tell me
options.description: You stop/pause the parallel session, then signal me — I'll restore + verify + commit once the repo is quiet. Safest.

options.label: It's done — proceed now
options.description: The parallel roster work is finished/idle; safe for me to restore the hourly lane and commit now without it being re-clobbered.

options.label: Don't know — investigate
options.description: Unsure what the other session is doing; I should dig further (which team/PID, what files it's targeting) before any write.
