tool_input.prompt:
Implement the CONSENSUS-APPROVED plan at `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/.omc/plans/sidebar-menu-simplify.md`. Read that plan in full first — it is the authoritative spec (it contains the exact target 25-leaf MODULES array, the test assertion diff, ACs, and a Precondition section). Work in `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend`.

SCOPE (content-only menu simplification; the 2-column rail+panel LAYOUT stays UNCHANGED — do NOT redesign the sidebar to an accordion):

1. **`src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx`** — Replace the `MODULES` array with the simplified 25-leaf set from the plan. IMPORTANT: read the CURRENT file first (it's in an intermediate edited state) and apply the plan's target. Net result must be:
   - Workspace (8): home, profile, time, leaves(badge 3), payslips, benefits(`__BENEFITS__`), documents, announce — **cut `requests`**
   - Team (5): approvals(`/quick-approve`, badge 12, label "กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ" / "Inbox · Approvals", MERGED from inbox+approvals), roster(`/roster`), perf(`/performance-form`), probation(`/manager-dashboard/probations`), reports(`/reports`) — **cut `swap`** (it's a modal inside Roster)
   - HR Admin (8): employees, orgchart, hire(merges lifecycle), recruit, benefits-admin(`/admin/benefits`, badge 2, merges welfare+claims), hr-docs(`/admin/documents`, merges confirm), changes(`/admin/change-requests`, merges transfer+regular), offboard(`/resignation`) — **cut comp/assets/attendance/audit-as-HR**
   - System (4): roles(`/permissions`), catalog(`/admin/foundation`), docreview(`/admin/documents` — documented Principle-1 exception, keep it), audit(`/admin/system`)
   - Preserve all `show` persona-gates, badges, and the `__BENEFITS__` sentinel exactly per the plan.
   - **Remove the `SIDEBAR_LEGACY` dead-code block.**
   - requests + comp are CUT (plan default).

2. **`src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx`** — Update per the plan's assertion diff: workspace now 8 (drop `ใบคำขอ`), drop the `ค่าตอบแทน → /payroll` assertion, keep/adjust the merged `กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ` assertions, add roster→/roster and probation→/manager-dashboard/probations, add absence assertions for cut leaves. The plan recommends renaming the file to `sidebar-ia.test.tsx` with an "intentionally diverges from SF/Blueprint parity" header — do the rename + header.

3. **`src/app/[locale]/time/timesheet/page.tsx`** — relabel the eyebrow per the plan so Timesheet reads as hour-logging (e.g. eyebrow "บันทึกชั่วโมงงาน" / hour-logging) and is not confusable with the Roster shift schedule. Keep it inline (no i18n key changes).

4. **Roster precondition (plan Precondition path 1):** the roster page `src/app/[locale]/roster/**` is git-UNTRACKED. `git add` those files (and `src/components/roster/**` if untracked) so the `roster → /roster` link is real on this branch. Do NOT commit — just stage (Ken owns commits/PRs).

VERIFY (must pass before reporting done):
- `npm test -- --run sidebar-ia` (or `sf-parity-sidebar` if you didn't rename) — sidebar test green
- `npm test -- --run i18n-completeness` — TH/EN parity gate green
- `npm run build` — clean (no TS errors; the MODULES change + any removed imports must not break build)

Humi tokens only; no hardcoded hex; no red/rose/pink classes. Do NOT touch the sidebar layout/CSS, AppShell, Topbar, or any file outside the 3 above (+ git-add roster). Report back: exact files changed, final leaf count per group, and the test/build results (paste the pass/fail summary lines).

tool_input.old_string:
/**
 * sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx — Blueprint sidebar IA structure + behaviour
 * Framework: Vitest + @testing-library/react + jsdom
 *
 * Ported 2026-05-25 from the HRMS Blueprint shell; the shell later became a
 * master-detail rail+panel (col-1 macro-group rail of role="tab" buttons, col-2
 * the selected group's leaves). This suite guards the SF-PARITY INTENT (leaf set,
 * group structure, role-gating + locked-state semantics that mirror SuccessFactors)
 * against the rail/panel API — the assertions below are intent, not just API shape.
 *
 * The 4 macro groups:
 *   A. พื้นที่ทำงานของฉัน (workspace) — 9 ESS leaves, visible to everyone
 *   B. การจัดการทีม (team)            — manager/HR/SPD tools (role-gated)
 *   C. งานบุคคล (hr)                  — HR-admin destinations (role-gated)
 *   D. ตั้งค่าระบบ (system)            — sysadmin settings (role-gated)
 *
 * KEY BEHAVIOURS asserted here (SF-parity intent):
 *  - All 4 group rail tabs ALWAYS render. A group with zero visible leaves for
 *    the persona is `locked` (disabled rail tab + .locked class — the rail has no
 *    accordion "—" count chrome; locked semantic carried by disabled/.locked).
 *  - Master-detail: exactly ONE group's leaves are visible at a time; the active
 *    route drives the default selection. (Replaces the old single-open accordion's
 *    toggle-to-collapse mechanic, which the rail/panel does not have by design.)
 *  - Leaves render as <Link>; active leaf derived from pathname (.is-active).
 *  - Persona→Role gating (PERSONA_ROLE in Sidebar.tsx):
 *      employee   → workspace only.
 *      manager    → unlocks team (roster/swap/probation/perf + inbox·approvals/reports).
 *      hr_admin   → unlocks team + hr (NOT system — see below).
 *      hr_manager → unlocks all four groups (sysadmin persona maps to hr_manager).
 *  - Req5 SIMPLIFY (2026-05-25): the menu was trimmed to 29 real leaves; every
 *    pure-decoration ?section= placeholder leaf was cut. The system group is now
 *    just Roles & Permissions + Integrations (both sysadmin-only), so hr_admin no
 *    longer unlocks system — its former system leaves were decoration cuts.
 */
tool_input.new_string:
/**
 * sidebar-ia.test.tsx — Humi sidebar IA structure + behaviour
 * Framework: Vitest + @testing-library/react + jsdom
 *
 * NOTE: this suite INTENTIONALLY DIVERGES from SF/Blueprint parity. The menu was
 * simplified 40 → 25 leaves per .omc/plans/sidebar-menu-simplify.md — placeholder /
 * deep-link leaves were CUT, not repointed. This file asserts the REDUCED IA (the
 * shipping contract), NOT the Blueprint's 40-leaf tree. Do not "fix" the menu back
 * toward Blueprint parity on the strength of this test. The shell remains the
 * master-detail rail+panel (col-1 macro-group rail of role="tab" buttons, col-2 the
 * selected group's leaves); the assertions below are IA intent, not just API shape.
 *
 * The 4 macro groups (reduced IA):
 *   A. พื้นที่ทำงานของฉัน (workspace) — 8 ESS leaves, visible to everyone (requests CUT)
 *   B. การจัดการทีม (team)            — 5 manager/HR/SPD tools (role-gated; swap CUT)
 *   C. งานบุคคล (hr)                  — 8 HR-admin destinations (role-gated; comp/assets cut)
 *   D. ตั้งค่าระบบ (system)            — 4 settings: Roles, Master Catalog, Document Review, Audit & System
 *
 * KEY BEHAVIOURS asserted here:
 *  - All 4 group rail tabs ALWAYS render. A group with zero visible leaves for
 *    the persona is `locked` (disabled rail tab + .locked class — the rail has no
 *    accordion "—" count chrome; locked semantic carried by disabled/.locked).
 *  - Master-detail: exactly ONE group's leaves are visible at a time; the active
 *    route drives the default selection. (Replaces the old single-open accordion's
 *    toggle-to-collapse mechanic, which the rail/panel does not have by design.)
 *  - Leaves render as <Link>; active leaf derived from pathname (.is-active).
 *  - Persona→Role gating (PERSONA_ROLE in Sidebar.tsx):
 *      employee   → workspace only.
 *      manager    → unlocks team (roster/probation/perf + inbox·approvals/reports).
 *      hr_admin   → unlocks team + hr + system (the `audit` leaf lists hradmin).
 *      hr_manager → unlocks all four groups (sysadmin persona maps to hr_manager).
 *  - Menu simplify (40→25): every pure-decoration ?section=/#tab placeholder leaf was
 *    cut. The system group is now Roles & Permissions + Master Catalog + Document
 *    Review + Audit & System; hr_admin unlocks it via the `audit` leaf.
 */

Make home quick-menu show 12 items
tool_input.prompt:
UI-mockup task in a Next.js HRMS (`src/frontend`). The home page "เมนูลัด" (Quick menu) must show the **12 canonical module shortcuts**. Right now it shows fewer (seed has 8, the user's persisted localStorage shows ~4).

## Root cause
- `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/home/page.tsx` renders `published.quickActions` from the `useAdminSelfService` zustand store (BRD #182 admin-configurable). It falls back to `DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS` only when `published.quickActions` is empty — but it's NOT empty (seeded), so the seed wins.
- The seed is `src/frontend/src/data/admin/mockAdminSelfService.json` → `quickActions` (currently 8 entries, not the canonical 12).
- The store persists `published` to localStorage key `admin-ss-config-v1` (in `src/frontend/src/lib/admin/store/useAdminSelfService.ts`), so stale browser state overrides the seed.

## The canonical 12 (source of truth)
`DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS` in `src/frontend/src/components/humi/QuickActionsTile.tsx` (read it). The 12, in order, with their Lucide icon + href:
1. ขอลาหยุด — CalendarPlus — /th/timeoff
2. สลิปเงินเดือน — FileText — /th/profile/me?tab=employment#pay-statements
3. ข้อมูลส่วนตัว — User — /th/profile/me
4. เบิกสวัสดิการ — Wallet — (use the exact string returned by `benefitReimbursementRoute('th')` — read `src/frontend/src/lib/benefit-routes.ts`)
5. ขอเอกสาร — FilePlus — /th/me/documents/request
6. บันทึกเวลา — Clock — /th/time-clock
7. คำขอของฉัน — Inbox — /th/requests
8. ประกาศ — Bell — /th/announcements
9. แผนผังองค์กร — Network — /th/org-chart
10. ทำเนียบพนักงาน — Users2 — /th/admin/employees
11. ผลงานของฉัน — BarChart3 — /th/performance
12. การเรียนรู้ — GraduationCap — /th/learning

## Changes
1. **Seed JSON** `src/frontend/src/data/admin/mockAdminSelfService.json`: replace the `quickActions` array with exactly these 12 entries. Each entry shape (match the existing entries): `{ "id": "<kebab/snake unique>", "label": "<Thai label above>", "icon": "<Lucide name above>", "href": "<href above>", "enabled": true, "order": <1..12> }`. Keep all other JSON keys (fieldConfig, visibility, tiles, audit, etc.) untouched.

2. **ICON_MAP** in `src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/home/page.tsx`: the map currently has FileText, FilePlus, MessageSquare, ThumbsUp, Network, BarChart3, CalendarPlus, Wallet, User. Add the missing ones used above: **Clock, Inbox, Bell, Users2, GraduationCap**. Add them to the `ICON_MAP` object AND to the `lucide-react` import in that file. (Leave MessageSquare/ThumbsUp in the map even though unused by the new seed — other code may reference; just ensure no unused-import lint error: if MessageSquare/ThumbsUp become unused imports, keep them only if already imported & referenced in ICON_MAP — they're referenced in ICON_MAP so they stay used.)

3. **Persist version bump** in `src/frontend/src/lib/admin/store/useAdminSelfService.ts`: change the persist `name` from `'admin-ss-config-v1'` to `'admin-ss-config-v2'` so stale localStorage (old quickActions) is discarded and the new 12-item seed loads on next visit. Add a brief comment noting the bump reseeds quick actions to the 12 module shortcuts.

## Constraints
- Mockup phase: no backend. `makeAdminQuickActions` uses `label` for both TH/EN — that's fine, leave it.
- Don't change `QuickActionsTile.tsx` or `DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS`.
- Follow existing JSON formatting/indentation.

## Verify before reporting (REQUIRED), from `src/frontend`:
1. `python3 -c "import json;d=json.load(open('src/data/admin/mockAdminSelfService.json'));qa=d['quickActions'];print('count',len(qa));[print(x['order'],x['label'],x['icon'],x['href']) for x in sorted(qa,key=lambda e:e['order'])]"` → must print count 12 and all 12 rows.
2. `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json 2>&1 | grep -iE "home/page|useAdminSelfService|mockAdminSelfService"` then `echo done` → no errors for these files.
3. Confirm the persist key is now `admin-ss-config-v2`: `grep -n "admin-ss-config" src/lib/admin/store/useAdminSelfService.ts`.

Report: files changed, the verify outputs (paste them), and any deviation. Do NOT commit.

tool_input.content:
---
name: feedback_rbac_menu_remove_not_hide
description: RBAC menus — if a role lacks access, remove the menu item/group entirely; never render it locked/disabled
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

If a persona/role has no access to a menu item or group, do NOT render it at all — remove it entirely. Never show a locked/disabled/greyed menu entry.

**Why:** A visible-but-locked menu implies access the user doesn't have and causes confusion ("ไม่ใช่แค่ซ่อน, กันเข้าใจผิด"). Only show what the role can actually enter.

**How to apply:** In `src/frontend/src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx`, `visibleGroups` filters out groups with zero accessible leaves (`.filter((g) => g.shownLeaves.length > 0)`) and the rail button has no `locked`/`disabled` state. Tests in `sidebar-ia.test.tsx` assert inaccessible groups are absent (`queryRailTab(...).not.toBeInTheDocument()`), not disabled. Apply the same remove-don't-lock rule to any future nav/permission surface. Related: [[feedback_sidebar_menu_simplify_cleanest]].
